======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_Status_1

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_Vehicle_Speed

ECU: IC
  TRANSMITS: IC_Driver_Inputs

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_Consolidated_Status

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: BCM_Status_1
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x100
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Hazard_Light_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Headlight_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 
    SIGNAL: Door_Lock_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Trunk_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Interior_Light_Level
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 100] %

MESSAGE: ECM_Vehicle_Speed
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x200
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Vehicle_Speed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h

MESSAGE: IC_Driver_Inputs
  Transmitter: IC
  CAN ID: 0x300
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Horn_Switch_Pressed
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: High_Beam_Switch_Requested
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Consolidated_Status
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0x400
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg
      Data Type: float (scale=0.5, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h
    SIGNAL: GWS_Any_Door_Open
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: GWS_Horn_Active
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1]

    # Initialize CANoe API and setup the simulation environment
    api = CanoeAPI()
    api.connect()
    api.load_simulation("bcm_simulation")
    yield api
    api.disconnect()

class TestBcmHazardLightStatus:

response.python_code:
import pytest
from canoe import CanoeAPI

@pytest.fixture
def canoe_setup():
    # Initialize CANoe API and setup simulation environment
    api = CanoeAPI()
    api.start_simulation("BCM")
    yield api
    api.stop_simulation()

class TestBcmHazardLightStatusTransmission:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("test_case", [
        {"test_id": "TC_001", "name": "Hazard Light On - Normal Operation"},
        {"test_id": "TC_002", "name": "Hazard Light Off - Normal Operation"},
        {"test_id": "TC_003", "name": "Hazard Light Status - Edge Case (Boundary Value)"},
        {"test_id": "TC_004", "name": "CAN Bus Error - Negative Test Case"},
        {"test_id": "TC_005", "name": "ECU Initialization - Timing/Sequence Test"},
        {"test_id": "TC_006", "name": "Hazard Light Status Transition - State Transition Test"},
        {"test_id": "TC_007", "name": "Hazard Light Status - Normal Operation (Multiple Times)"}
    ])
    def test_hazard_light_status_transmission(self, canoe_setup, test_case):
        # Set up simulation environment
        api = canoe_setup

        # Perform test steps
        for step in test_case["steps"]:
            if step["action"] == "Turn Hazard Lights On":
                api.send_message("Hazard_Light_Status", 1)
            elif step["action"] == "Turn Hazard Lights Off":
                api.send_message("Hazard_Light_Status", 0)
            elif step["action"] == "Check CAN Bus for Message":
                received_messages = api.get_received_messages()
                assert len(received_messages) > 0
                message = received_messages[0]
                if test_case["test_id"] in ["TC_001", "TC_003", "TC_007"]:
                    assert message["Hazard_Light_Status"] == 1
                else:
                    assert message["Hazard_Light_Status"] == 0

    def test_hazard_light_status_transmission_multiple_times(self, canoe_setup):
        # Set up simulation environment
        api = canoe_setup

        # Perform test steps
        for _ in range(5):  # Turn hazard lights on and off multiple times
            api.send_message("Hazard_Light_Status", 1)
            received_messages = api.get_received_messages()
            assert len(received_messages) > 0
            message = received_messages[0]
            assert message["Hazard_Light_Status"] == 1

        # Check that hazard lights are still on after multiple turns
        received_messages = api.get_received_messages()
        assert len(received_messages) > 0
        message = received_messages[0]
        assert message["Hazard_Light_Status"] == 1

import pytest
from canoe import CanoeAPI
from capl import CAPLScript

@pytest.fixture
def canoe_setup():
    # Initialize CANoe API and CAPL script
    api = CanoeAPI()
    script = CAPLScript("bcm_script.capl")
    return api, script

class TestHazardLightStatus:
    def test_hazard_light_status_transmitted(self, canoe_setup):
        api, script = canoe_setup
        # Set hazard light status to 1 and send message
        msg = script.get_message("BCM_Status_1")
        msg.Hazard_Light_Status = 1
        api.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages on CAN bus
        received_msgs = api.get_received_messages()
        
        # Check if BCM_Status_1 message is received with correct hazard light status
        assert len(received_msgs) == 1
        assert received_msgs[0].Hazard_Light_Status == 1

    def test_hazard_light_status_invalid_value(self, canoe_setup):
        api, script = canoe_setup
        # Set hazard light status to an invalid value (2) and send message
        msg = script.get_message("BCM_Status_1")
        msg.Hazard_Light_Status = 2
        api.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages on CAN bus
        received_msgs = api.get_received_messages()
        
        # Check if no BCM_Status_1 message is received (invalid hazard light status)
        assert len(received_msgs) == 0

    def test_hazard_light_status_out_of_range(self, canoe_setup):
        api, script = canoe_setup
        # Set hazard light status to an out of range value (-1) and send message
        msg = script.get_message("BCM_Status_1")
        msg.Hazard_Light_Status = -1
        api.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages on CAN bus
        received_msgs = api.get_received_messages()
        
        # Check if no BCM_Status_1 message is received (out of range hazard light status)
        assert len(received_msgs) == 0

    def test_hazard_light_status_very_large_value(self, canoe_setup):
        api, script = canoe_setup
        # Set hazard light status to a very large value (1000000) and send message
        msg = script.get_message("BCM_Status_1")
        msg.Hazard_Light_Status = 1000000
        api.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages on CAN bus
        received_msgs = api.get_received_messages()
        
        # Check if no BCM_Status_1 message is received (very large hazard light status)
        assert len(received_msgs) == 0

    def test_hazard_light_status_invalid_value_cyclic(self, canoe_setup):
        api, script = canoe_setup
        # Set hazard light status to an invalid value (2) and send message in cyclic mode
        msg = script.get_message("BCM_Status_1")
        msg.Hazard_Light_Status = 2
        api.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages on CAN bus
        received_msgs = api.get_received_messages()
        
        # Check if no BCM_Status_1 message is received (invalid hazard light status in cyclic mode)
        assert len(received_msgs) == 0

    def test_hazard_light_status_out_of_range_cyclic(self, canoe_setup):
        api, script = canoe_setup
        # Set hazard light status to an out of range value (-1) and send message in cyclic mode
        msg = script.get_message("BCM_Status_1")
        msg.Hazard_Light_Status = -1
        api.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages on CAN bus
        received_msgs = api.get_received_messages()
        
        # Check if no BCM_Status_1 message is received (out of range hazard light status in cyclic mode)
        assert len(received_msgs) == 0

    def test_hazard_light_status_very_large_value_cyclic(self, canoe_setup):
        api, script = canoe_setup
        # Set hazard light status to a very large value (1000000) and send message in cyclic mode
        msg = script.get_message("BCM_Status_1")
        msg.Hazard_Light_Status = 1000000
        api.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages on CAN bus
        received_msgs = api.get_received_messages()
        
        # Check if no BCM_Status_1 message is received (very large hazard light status in cyclic mode)
        assert len(received_msgs) == 0

**Expected Result**: BCM sends BCM_Status_1 message with Hazard_Light_Status = 0 on CAN bus

## TC_003: CAN Bus Error - Negative Test

**Description**: Test BCM behavior when there is a CAN bus error

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active with error']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Simulate CAN bus error', 'signal': None, 'value': None, 'description': 'Introduce a CAN bus error'}
- {'action': 'Verify BCM does not send BCM_Status_1 message on CAN bus', 'signal': 'BCM_Status_1', 'value': None, 'description': 'Check if BCM does not send the status message due to CAN bus error'}

**Description**: Test BCM transmitting hazard light status at boundary value (0 or 1)

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Expected Result**: BCM does not send BCM_Status_1 message on CAN bus when hazard light status is out of range (less than 0 or greater than 1)

## TC_008: Hazard Light Status Edge Case - Very Large Value

**Description**: Test BCM behavior when hazard light status has a very large value

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

- {'action': 'Set Hazard Light Status to 1000000 (very large value)', 'signal': 'Hazard_Light_Status', 'value': 1000000, 'description': 'Try to set hazard light status to a very large value'}
- {'action': 'Verify BCM does not send BCM_Status_1 message on CAN bus', 'signal': 'BCM_Status_1', 'value': None, 'description': 'Check if BCM does not send the status message due to hazard light status being too large'}

**Expected Result**: BCM does not send BCM_Status_1 message on CAN bus when hazard light status has a very large value

import pytest
from canoe import CanoeAPI

@pytest.fixture
def canoe_setup():
    # Initialize CANoe API
    canoe = CanoeAPI()
    
    # Set up test environment
    canoe.set_test_environment("GW Door Lock Status Test Suite")
    
    yield canoe
    
    # Clean up test environment
    canoe.cleanup()

class TestGWSpeedWarning:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("test_case", [
        {"test_id": "TC_001", "name": "Door Locked and Trunk Open"},
        {"test_id": "TC_002", "name": "Door Unlocked and Trunk Closed"},
        {"test_id": "TC_003", "name": "Door Locked and Trunk Closed"},
        {"test_id": "TC_004", "name": "Trunk Open and Door Unlocked"}
    ])
    def test_positive(self, canoe_setup, test_case):
        # Send BCM_Status_1 message with correct CAN ID
        canoe_setup.send_message(256, {"Door_Lock_Status": 1, "Trunk_Status": 1})
        
        # Check if GWS_Any_Door_Open is set to 1
        received_messages = canoe_setup.get_received_messages()
        assert len(received_messages) > 0
        msgGWConsolidatedStatus = received_messages[0]
        assert msgGWConsolidatedStatus["GWS_Any_Door_Open"] == 1
    
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("test_case", [
        {"test_id": "TC_005", "name": "Invalid CAN ID"}
    ])
    def test_negative(self, canoe_setup, test_case):
        # Send BCM_Status_1 message with incorrect CAN ID
        canoe_setup.send_message(255, {"Door_Lock_Status": 1})
        
        # Check if GWS_Any_Door_Open is not set to 1
        received_messages = canoe_setup.get_received_messages()
        assert len(received_messages) > 0
        msgGWConsolidatedStatus = received_messages[0]
        assert msgGWConsolidatedStatus["GWS_Any_Door_Open"] == 0
    
    def test_no_message(self, canoe_setup):
        # Send no BCM_Status_1 message
        
        # Check if GWS_Any_Door_Open is not set to 1
        received_messages = canoe_setup.get_received_messages()
        assert len(received_messages) == 0

# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Door Locked and Trunk Open

**Description**: Test if GW sets GWS_Any_Door_Open to 1 when Door_Lock_Status is Locked (1) OR Trunk_Status is Open (1)

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status_1 message', 'signal': 'Door_Lock_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Set Door_Lock_Status to Locked (1)'}
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status_1 message', 'signal': 'Trunk_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Set Trunk_Status to Open (1)'}

**Expected Result**: GWS_Any_Door_Open should be set to 1

## TC_002: Door Unlocked and Trunk Closed

**Description**: Test if GW does not set GWS_Any_Door_Open when Door_Lock_Status is Unlocked (0) AND Trunk_Status is Closed (0)

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status_1 message', 'signal': 'Door_Lock_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set Door_Lock_Status to Unlocked (0)'}
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status_1 message', 'signal': 'Trunk_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set Trunk_Status to Closed (0)'}

**Expected Result**: GWS_Any_Door_Open should not be set

## TC_003: Door Locked and Trunk Closed

**Description**: Test if GW does not set GWS_Any_Door_Open when Door_Lock_Status is Locked (1) AND Trunk_Status is Closed (0)

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status_1 message', 'signal': 'Door_Lock_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Set Door_Lock_Status to Locked (1)'}
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status_1 message', 'signal': 'Trunk_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set Trunk_Status to Closed (0)'}

**Expected Result**: GWS_Any_Door_Open should not be set

## TC_004: Trunk Open and Door Unlocked

**Description**: Test if GW sets GWS_Any_Door_Open to 1 when Trunk_Status is Open (1) AND Door_Lock_Status is Unlocked (0)

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status_1 message', 'signal': 'Trunk_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Set Trunk_Status to Open (1)'}
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status_1 message', 'signal': 'Door_Lock_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set Door_Lock_Status to Unlocked (0)'}

**Expected Result**: GWS_Any_Door_Open should be set to 1

## TC_005: Invalid CAN ID

**Description**: Test if GW ignores invalid BCM_Status_1 message with CAN ID other than 256

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status_1 message with CAN ID 255', 'signal': 'Door_Lock_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Set Door_Lock_Status to Locked (1) in an invalid message'}

**Expected Result**: GWS_Any_Door_Open should not be set

## TC_006: No BCM_Status_1 message received

**Description**: Test if GW does not set GWS_Any_Door_Open when no BCM_Status_1 message is received

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'No BCM_Status_1 message sent'}

**Expected Result**: GWS_Any_Door_Open should not be set

user_input.requirement: When the signal 'Headlight Status' is active, the system shall reflect the corresponding condition in the vehicle user interface or gateway indicators.
user_input.dbc_content:
======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_Status_1

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_Vehicle_Speed

ECU: IC
  TRANSMITS: IC_Driver_Inputs

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_Consolidated_Status

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: BCM_Status_1
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x100
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Hazard_Light_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Headlight_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 
    SIGNAL: Door_Lock_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Trunk_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Interior_Light_Level
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 100] %

MESSAGE: ECM_Vehicle_Speed
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x200
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Vehicle_Speed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h

MESSAGE: IC_Driver_Inputs
  Transmitter: IC
  CAN ID: 0x300
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Horn_Switch_Pressed
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: High_Beam_Switch_Requested
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Consolidated_Status
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0x400
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg
      Data Type: float (scale=0.5, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h
    SIGNAL: GWS_Any_Door_Open
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: GWS_Horn_Active
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1]

=== CAPL EXAMPLES ===
--- CAPL EXAMPLES #1 ---
on message IC_Driver_Command
{
  turnCommand = this.IC_Turn_Command;
  updateExteriorLightState();
}

void updateExteriorLightState()
{
  int anyLight = 0; int indicatorState = 0;
  if (hazardActive == 1 || turnCommand == 3) { indicatorState = 3; anyLight = 1; }
  else if (turnCommand == 1) { indicatorState = 1; anyLight = 1; }
  else if (turnCommand == 2) { indicatorState = 2; anyLight = 1; }
  if (parkingLamp == 1) anyLight = 1;
  msgGW.GWS_Any_Light_On = anyLight;
  msgGW.GWS_Indicator_State = indicatorState;
  output(msgGW);
}

      "offset": 0,
      "unit": ""
    }
  ]
}

=== CAPL EXAMPLES ===
--- CAPL EXAMPLES #1 ---
on message IC_Driver_Command
{
  turnCommand = this.IC_Turn_Command;
  updateExteriorLightState();
}

void updateExteriorLightState()
{
  int anyLight = 0; int indicatorState = 0;
  if (hazardActive == 1 || turnCommand == 3) { indicatorState = 3; anyLight = 1; }
  else if (turnCommand == 1) { indicatorState = 1; anyLight = 1; }
  else if (turnCommand == 2) { indicatorState = 2; anyLight = 1; }
  if (parkingLamp == 1) anyLight = 1;
  msgGW.GWS_Any_Light_On = anyLight;
  msgGW.GWS_Indicator_State = indicatorState;
  output(msgGW);
}

--- CAPL EXAMPLES #2 ---
on message IC_Driver_Command
{
  if (this.IC_Horn_Request != hornState)
  {
    hornState = this.IC_Horn_Request;
    gwMsg.GWS_Horn_Active = hornState;
    output(gwMsg);
  }

response.test_scenario:
# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Normal Operation - Headlights On

**Description**: Test normal operation of headlight control system with headlights on.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active', 'Headlights are turned on']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Message to GW', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 120, 'description': 'Simulate vehicle speed'}
- {'action': 'Check Headlight Status', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify headlight status is on'}

**Expected Result**: Headlights are turned on and functioning correctly.

## TC_002: Error Condition - CAN Bus Fault

**Description**: Test error condition of headlight control system with CAN bus fault.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active', 'Simulate CAN bus fault']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Message to GW', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 120, 'description': 'Simulate vehicle speed'}
- {'action': 'Check Error Status', 'signal': 'Error_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify error status is set'}

**Expected Result**: Headlight control system enters fault mode.

## TC_003: Edge Case - Vehicle Speed at Maximum

**Description**: Test edge case of headlight control system with vehicle speed at maximum.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active', 'Vehicle speed is at maximum']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Message to GW', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 300, 'description': 'Simulate vehicle speed'}
- {'action': 'Check Headlight Status', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify headlight status is on'}

**Expected Result**: Headlights are turned on and functioning correctly at maximum vehicle speed.

## TC_004: Timing/Sequence Test - Headlight Turn-On Delay

**Description**: Test timing/sequence of headlight control system with headlight turn-on delay.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Message to GW', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 120, 'description': 'Simulate vehicle speed'}
- {'action': 'Check Headlight Status after Delay', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify headlight status is on after delay'}

**Expected Result**: Headlights turn on after specified delay.

## TC_005: State Transition Test - Headlight Turn-Off

**Description**: Test state transition of headlight control system with headlight turn-off.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Message to GW', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Simulate vehicle speed'}
- {'action': 'Check Headlight Status', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify headlight status is off'}

**Expected Result**: Headlights are turned off and functioning correctly.

## TC_006: State Transition Test - Headlight Turn-On from Off

**Description**: Test state transition of headlight control system with headlight turn-on from off.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Message to GW', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 120, 'description': 'Simulate vehicle speed'}
- {'action': 'Check Headlight Status', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify headlight status is on'}

**Expected Result**: Headlights are turned on and functioning correctly.

user_input.dbc_content:
======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_Status_1

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_Vehicle_Speed

ECU: IC
  TRANSMITS: IC_Driver_Inputs

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_Consolidated_Status

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: BCM_Status_1
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x100
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Hazard_Light_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Headlight_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 
    SIGNAL: Door_Lock_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Trunk_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Interior_Light_Level
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 100] %

MESSAGE: ECM_Vehicle_Speed
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x200
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Vehicle_Speed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h

MESSAGE: IC_Driver_Inputs
  Transmitter: IC
  CAN ID: 0x300
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Horn_Switch_Pressed
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: High_Beam_Switch_Requested
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Consolidated_Status
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0x400
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg
      Data Type: float (scale=0.5, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h
    SIGNAL: GWS_Any_Door_Open
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: GWS_Horn_Active
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1]

response.python_code:
import pytest
from capl import canoe_api as ca

@pytest.fixture
def setup_canoe():
    # Initialize CANoe API
    ca.init()
    
    # Connect to the ECU under test
    ca.connect_to_ecu("ECUUnderTest")
    
    yield
    
    # Disconnect from the ECU and clean up
    ca.disconnect_from_ecu()
    ca.cleanup()

@pytest.fixture
def can_bus(setup_canoe):
    return ca.get_can_bus()

class TestSpeedWarning:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("test_id, name", [
        ("TC_001", "Normal Operation - Vehicle Speed"),
        ("TC_002", "Error Condition - Invalid Vehicle Speed"),
        ("TC_003", "Edge Case - Vehicle Speed at Maximum Value"),
        ("TC_004", "Timing/Sequence Test - Vehicle Speed Update Interval"),
        ("TC_005", "State Transition Test - Vehicle Speed Change"),
        ("TC_006", "Edge Case - Vehicle Speed at Minimum Value")
    ])
    def test_tc_speed_warning(self, can_bus, test_id, name):
        # Send GW Consolidated Status message
        msg = ca.create_message("GW Consolidated Status")
        msg.set_signal_value("GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg", 120)
        ca.send_message(can_bus, msg)
        
        # Wait for update interval (e.g., 100ms)
        import time
        time.sleep(0.1)
        
        # Get received messages
        received_msgs = can_bus.get_received_messages()
        
        if test_id == "TC_001":
            assert len(received_msgs) > 0
            assert received_msgs[0].get_signal_value("GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg") == 120
        elif test_id == "TC_002":
            assert len(received_msgs) > 0
            assert received_msgs[0].get_signal_value("GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg") != 500
        elif test_id == "TC_003":
            assert len(received_msgs) > 0
            assert received_msgs[0].get_signal_value("GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg") == 300
        elif test_id == "TC_004":
            # Check if messages are sent at correct intervals with updated vehicle speed data
            for i, msg in enumerate(received_msgs):
                if i % 10 == 0:
                    assert msg.get_signal_value("GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg") == 120
        elif test_id == "TC_005":
            # Check if messages are sent with correct vehicle speed value after transition
            for msg in received_msgs:
                assert msg.get_signal_value("GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg") != 0
        elif test_id == "TC_006":
            assert len(received_msgs) > 0
            assert received_msgs[0].get_signal_value("GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg") == 0

    def test_tc_hl_003_transition(self, can_bus):
        # Wait for next BCM message
        msg = ca.wait_for_next_bcm_message(can_bus)
        
        # Get Headlight Status from the message
        headlight_status = ca.get_headlight_status(msg.data)
        
        assert headlight_status == 3

    def test_tc_hb_003_transition_off_to_on(self, can_bus):
        # Wait for next IC frame
        msg = ca.wait_for_next_ic_frame(can_bus)
        
        # Get High Beam Status from the message
        high_beam_status = ca.get_high_beam_status(msg.data)
        
        assert high_beam_status == 1
response.test_scenario:
# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Normal Operation - Vehicle Speed

**Description**: Test normal operation of GW Consolidated Status with valid vehicle speed data.

**Preconditions**: ['GW ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send GW Consolidated Status message', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 120, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to 120 km/h'}

**Expected Result**: GW Consolidated Status message sent successfully with correct vehicle speed data.

## TC_002: Error Condition - Invalid Vehicle Speed

**Description**: Test error handling when GW Consolidated Status receives invalid vehicle speed data.

**Preconditions**: ['GW ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send GW Consolidated Status message with invalid vehicle speed', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 500, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to an invalid value (above maximum)'}

**Expected Result**: GW ECU raises error and logs the issue

## TC_003: Edge Case - Vehicle Speed at Maximum Value

**Description**: Test edge case where vehicle speed is at its maximum value.

**Preconditions**: ['GW ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send GW Consolidated Status message with vehicle speed at maximum value', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 300, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to its maximum allowed value'}

**Expected Result**: GW Consolidated Status message sent successfully with correct vehicle speed data.

## TC_004: Timing/Sequence Test - Vehicle Speed Update Interval

**Description**: Test timing and sequence of GW Consolidated Status updates for vehicle speed.

**Preconditions**: ['GW ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send GW Consolidated Status message with initial vehicle speed value', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 120, 'description': 'Set initial vehicle speed to 120 km/h'}
- {'action': 'Wait for update interval (e.g., 100ms)', 'description': 'Wait for the next update interval'}
- {'action': 'Send GW Consolidated Status message with updated vehicle speed value', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 130, 'description': 'Set updated vehicle speed to 130 km/h'}

**Expected Result**: GW Consolidated Status messages sent successfully at correct intervals with updated vehicle speed data.

## TC_005: State Transition Test - Vehicle Speed Change

**Description**: Test state transition of GW Consolidated Status when vehicle speed changes.

**Preconditions**: ['GW ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send GW Consolidated Status message with initial vehicle speed value', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 120, 'description': 'Set initial vehicle speed to 120 km/h'}
- {'action': 'Change vehicle speed to a new value (e.g., 150)', 'description': 'Change the actual vehicle speed to a new value'}
- {'action': 'Send GW Consolidated Status message with updated vehicle speed value', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 150, 'description': 'Set updated vehicle speed to 150 km/h'}

**Expected Result**: GW Consolidated Status messages sent successfully with correct vehicle speed data reflecting the state transition.

## TC_006: Edge Case - Vehicle Speed at Minimum Value

**Description**: Test edge case where vehicle speed is at its minimum value.

**Preconditions**: ['GW ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send GW Consolidated Status message with vehicle speed at minimum value', 'signal': 'GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to its minimum allowed value'}

**Expected Result**: GW Consolidated Status message sent successfully with correct vehicle speed data.

======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_VehicleData

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_BodyStatus

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_VehicleStatus, GW_Warnings

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: ECM_VehicleData
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x64
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: VehicleSpeed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h
    SIGNAL: EngineRPM
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8000] rpm

MESSAGE: BCM_BodyStatus
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x6E
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: DriverDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: PassengerDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: LeftIndicator
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: RightIndicator
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_VehicleStatus
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0xC8
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GW_SpeedOut
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h
    SIGNAL: AnyDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: TurnActive
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Warnings
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0xD2
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: OverspeedWarning
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: DoorWarning
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=No Warning, 1=Warning

variables
{
  message BCM_Body_State bcmMsg;
  message GW_System_Overview gwMsg;
  int anyDoorOpen;
}

on message BCM_Body_State
{
  bcmMsg = this;
  anyDoorOpen = (bcmMsg.Bdy_Door_Front_Left || bcmMsg.Bdy_Door_Front_Right || bcmMsg.Bdy_Door_Rear_Left || bcmMsg.Bdy_Door_Rear_Right);
  gwMsg.GWS_Any_Door_Open = anyDoorOpen;
  output(gwMsg);
}

response.python_code:
import pytest
from canoe import Canoe, Message

@pytest.fixture
def can_bus():
    return Canoe()

class TestGatewayConsolidatedDoorStatus:
    def test_tc_001_all_doors_closed(self, can_bus):
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with all doors closed
        msg = Message('BCM_BodyStatus')
        msg['DriverDoorOpen'] = 0
        msg['PassengerDoorOpen'] = 0
        can_bus.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages
        msgs = can_bus.get_received_messages('GW_VehicleStatus', 1)
        
        # Assert that the consolidated door status is correct
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        assert msgs[0]['AnyDoorOpen'] == 0

    def test_tc_002_one_door_open(self, can_bus):
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with one door open
        msg = Message('BCM_BodyStatus')
        msg['DriverDoorOpen'] = 1
        msg['PassengerDoorOpen'] = 0
        can_bus.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages
        msgs = can_bus.get_received_messages('GW_VehicleStatus', 1)
        
        # Assert that the consolidated door status is correct
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        assert msgs[0]['AnyDoorOpen'] == 1

    def test_tc_003_both_doors_open(self, can_bus):
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with both doors open
        msg = Message('BCM_BodyStatus')
        msg['DriverDoorOpen'] = 1
        msg['PassengerDoorOpen'] = 1
        can_bus.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages
        msgs = can_bus.get_received_messages('GW_VehicleStatus', 1)
        
        # Assert that the consolidated door status is correct
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        assert msgs[0]['AnyDoorOpen'] == 1

    def test_tc_004_invalid_door_status(self, can_bus):
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with an invalid door status
        msg = Message('BCM_BodyStatus')
        msg['DriverDoorOpen'] = 2
        can_bus.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages
        msgs = can_bus.get_received_messages('GW_VehicleStatus', 1)
        
        # Assert that the consolidated door status is correct (default or previous value)
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        assert msgs[0]['AnyDoorOpen'] == 0

    def test_tc_005_door_status_change(self, can_bus):
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with the door closed
        msg = Message('BCM_BodyStatus')
        msg['DriverDoorOpen'] = 0
        can_bus.send_message(msg)
        
        # Wait for 100ms
        import time
        time.sleep(0.1)
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with the door open
        msg['DriverDoorOpen'] = 1
        can_bus.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages within 100ms of the second message
        msgs = can_bus.get_received_messages('GW_VehicleStatus', 2, timeout=0.1)
        
        # Assert that the consolidated door status is correct and updated in a timely manner
        assert len(msgs) == 2
        assert msgs[1]['AnyDoorOpen'] == 1

    def test_tc_006_door_open_close_cycle(self, can_bus):
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with the door closed
        msg = Message('BCM_BodyStatus')
        msg['DriverDoorOpen'] = 0
        can_bus.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages
        msgs = can_bus.get_received_messages('GW_VehicleStatus', 1)
        
        # Assert that the consolidated door status is correct (door closed)
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        assert msgs[0]['AnyDoorOpen'] == 0
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with the door open
        msg['DriverDoorOpen'] = 1
        can_bus.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages
        msgs = can_bus.get_received_messages('GW_VehicleStatus', 1)
        
        # Assert that the consolidated door status is correct (door open)
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        assert msgs[0]['AnyDoorOpen'] == 1
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with the door closed again
        msg['DriverDoorOpen'] = 0
        can_bus.send_message(msg)
        
        # Get received messages
        msgs = can_bus.get_received_messages('GW_VehicleStatus', 1)
        
        # Assert that the consolidated door status is correct (door closed again)
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        assert msgs[0]['AnyDoorOpen'] == 0
response.test_scenario:
# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Normal Operation - All Doors Closed

**Description**: Test the gateway's ability to derive a consolidated door status when all doors are closed.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'DriverDoorOpen', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set driver door to closed'}
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'PassengerDoorOpen', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set passenger door to closed'}

**Expected Result**: GW_VehicleStatus message with AnyDoorOpen = 0

## TC_002: Normal Operation - One Door Open

**Description**: Test the gateway's ability to derive a consolidated door status when one door is open.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'DriverDoorOpen', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Set driver door to open'}
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'PassengerDoorOpen', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set passenger door to closed'}

**Expected Result**: GW_VehicleStatus message with AnyDoorOpen = 1

## TC_003: Edge Case - Both Doors Open

**Description**: Test the gateway's ability to derive a consolidated door status when both doors are open.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'DriverDoorOpen', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Set driver door to open'}
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'PassengerDoorOpen', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Set passenger door to open'}

**Expected Result**: GW_VehicleStatus message with AnyDoorOpen = 1

## TC_004: Negative Test - Invalid Door Status

**Description**: Test the gateway's ability to handle an invalid door status.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'DriverDoorOpen', 'value': 2, 'description': 'Set driver door to an invalid value'}

**Expected Result**: GW_VehicleStatus message with AnyDoorOpen = 0 (default or previous value)

## TC_005: Timing Test - Door Status Change

**Description**: Test the gateway's ability to update the consolidated door status in a timely manner.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'DriverDoorOpen', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set driver door to closed'}
- {'action': 'Wait for 100ms', 'description': 'Allow the gateway to process the previous message'}
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'DriverDoorOpen', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Set driver door to open'}

**Expected Result**: GW_VehicleStatus message with AnyDoorOpen = 1 within 100ms of the second BCM_BodyStatus message

## TC_006: State Transition Test - Door Open/Close Cycle

**Description**: Test the gateway's ability to handle a door open/close cycle.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'DriverDoorOpen', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set driver door to closed'}
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'DriverDoorOpen', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Set driver door to open'}
- {'action': 'Send BCM_BodyStatus message', 'signal': 'DriverDoorOpen', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set driver door to closed again'}

**Expected Result**: GW_VehicleStatus message with AnyDoorOpen = 1 when the door is open, and AnyDoorOpen = 0 when the door is closed

user_input.dbc_content:
======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_VehicleData

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_BodyStatus

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_VehicleStatus, GW_Warnings

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: ECM_VehicleData
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x64
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: VehicleSpeed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h
    SIGNAL: EngineRPM
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8000] rpm

MESSAGE: BCM_BodyStatus
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x6E
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: DriverDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: PassengerDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: LeftIndicator
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: RightIndicator
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_VehicleStatus
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0xC8
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GW_SpeedOut
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h
    SIGNAL: AnyDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: TurnActive
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Warnings
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0xD2
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: OverspeedWarning
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: DoorWarning
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=No Warning, 1=Warning

import pytest
from can import Message
from can.bus import BusState
from can.io import SocketCANBus

@pytest.fixture
def can_bus():
    bus = SocketCANBus(channel='vcan0', interface='socketcan')
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

class TestSpeedWarning:
    def test_tc_001_normal_operation(self, can_bus):
        # Preconditions
        simulate_ecm_initialized(can_bus)
        simulate_can_bus_active(can_bus)
        simulate_gw_initialized(can_bus)

        # Steps
        send_ecm_vehicle_data_message(can_bus, 50.0)
        gw_vehicle_status_messages = collect_gw_vehicle_status_messages(can_bus, 1.0)

        # Expected result
        assert len(gw_vehicle_status_messages) == 1
        assert gw_vehicle_status_messages[0].GW_SpeedOut == 50.0

    def test_tc_002_error_condition_invalid_vehicle_speed(self, can_bus):
        # Preconditions
        simulate_ecm_initialized(can_bus)
        simulate_can_bus_active(can_bus)
        simulate_gw_initialized(can_bus)

        # Steps
        send_ecm_vehicle_data_message(can_bus, 300.0)
        gw_vehicle_status_messages = collect_gw_vehicle_status_messages(can_bus, 1.0)

        # Expected result
        assert len(gw_vehicle_status_messages) == 0

    def test_tc_003_edge_case_minimum_vehicle_speed(self, can_bus):
        # Preconditions
        simulate_ecm_initialized(can_bus)
        simulate_can_bus_active(can_bus)
        simulate_gw_initialized(can_bus)

        # Steps
        send_ecm_vehicle_data_message(can_bus, 0.0)
        gw_vehicle_status_messages = collect_gw_vehicle_status_messages(can_bus, 1.0)

        # Expected result
        assert len(gw_vehicle_status_messages) == 1
        assert gw_vehicle_status_messages[0].GW_SpeedOut == 0.0

    def test_tc_004_timing_test_vehicle_speed_update_interval(self, can_bus):
        # Preconditions
        simulate_ecm_initialized(can_bus)
        simulate_can_bus_active(can_bus)
        simulate_gw_initialized(can_bus)

        # Steps
        send_ecm_vehicle_data_message(can_bus, 50.0)
        wait_for_time(can_bus, 100)
        send_ecm_vehicle_data_message(can_bus, 60.0)
        gw_vehicle_status_messages = collect_gw_vehicle_status_messages(can_bus, 1.0)

        # Expected result
        assert len(gw_vehicle_status_messages) == 2
        assert gw_vehicle_status_messages[0].GW_SpeedOut == 50.0
        assert gw_vehicle_status_messages[1].GW_SpeedOut == 60.0

    def test_tc_005_state_transition_test_vehicle_speed_change(self, can_bus):
        # Preconditions
        simulate_ecm_initialized(can_bus)
        simulate_can_bus_active(can_bus)
        simulate_gw_initialized(can_bus)

        # Steps
        send_ecm_vehicle_data_message(can_bus, 50.0)
        gw_vehicle_status_messages = collect_gw_vehicle_status_messages(can_bus, 1.0)
        send_ecm_vehicle_data_message(can_bus, 60.0)
        updated_gw_vehicle_status_messages = collect_gw_vehicle_status_messages(can_bus, 1.0)

        # Expected result
        assert len(gw_vehicle_status_messages) == 1
        assert gw_vehicle_status_messages[0].GW_SpeedOut == 50.0
        assert len(updated_gw_vehicle_status_messages) == 1
        assert updated_gw_vehicle_status_messages[0].GW_SpeedOut == 60.0

    def test_tc_006_error_condition_missing_ecm_vehicle_data_message(self, can_bus):
        # Preconditions
        simulate_ecm_initialized(can_bus)
        simulate_can_bus_active(can_bus)
        simulate_gw_initialized(can_bus)

        # Steps
        gw_vehicle_status_messages = collect_gw_vehicle_status_messages(can_bus, 1.0)

        # Expected result
        assert len(gw_vehicle_status_messages) == 0

def send_ecm_vehicle_data_message(can_bus, vehicle_speed):
    msg = Message(arbitration_id=0x123, data=b'\x00\x00\x00\x00')
    msg.data[0] = int(vehicle_speed)
    can_bus.send(msg)

def collect_gw_vehicle_status_messages(can_bus, timeout):
    messages = []
    start_time = time.time()
    while time.time() - start_time < timeout:
        msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg is not None and msg.arbitration_id == 0x456:
            messages.append(msg)
    return messages

def simulate_ecm_initialized(can_bus):
    # Simulate ECM initialization
    pass

def simulate_can_bus_active(can_bus):
    # Simulate CAN bus activation
    can_bus.state = BusState.ACTIVE

def simulate_gw_initialized(can_bus):
    # Simulate GW initialization
    pass

def wait_for_time(can_bus, time_ms):
    time.sleep(time_ms / 1000)

======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_VehicleData

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_BodyStatus

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_VehicleStatus, GW_Warnings

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: ECM_VehicleData
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x64
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: VehicleSpeed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h
    SIGNAL: EngineRPM
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8000] rpm

MESSAGE: BCM_BodyStatus
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x6E
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: DriverDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: PassengerDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: LeftIndicator
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: RightIndicator
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_VehicleStatus
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0xC8
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GW_SpeedOut
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h
    SIGNAL: AnyDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: TurnActive
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Warnings
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0xD2
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: OverspeedWarning
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: DoorWarning
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=No Warning, 1=Warning

--- SIMILAR REQUIREMENTS #2 ---
When one or more door open indications are detected from the body controller, the gateway shall immediately transmit a consolidated door-open status indicating whether any vehicle door is open.

--- SIMILAR REQUIREMENTS #3 ---
When any door (front or rear) transitions between open and closed states, the body controller shall immediately transmit the corresponding door status to the gateway ECU.

import pytest
from can import Message
from can.bus import BusState
from can.io import SocketCANBus

@pytest.fixture
def can_bus():
    bus = SocketCANBus(channel='vcan0', receive_own_messages=True)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

class TestDoorWarning:
    def test_tc_001_door_open_while_vehicle_in_motion(self, can_bus):
        # Send ECM_VehicleData message with non-zero vehicle speed
        msg_ecm_vehicle_data = Message(arbitration_id=0x123, data=b'\x00\x00\x32\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_ecm_vehicle_data)
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with driver door open status set to open
        msg_bcm_body_status = Message(arbitration_id=0x456, data=b'\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_bcm_body_status)
        
        # Collect received messages
        msgs = can_bus.recv(1)
        
        # Assert GW_Warnings message with DoorWarning signal set to 1 (Warning)
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        msg_gw_warnings = msgs[0]
        assert msg_gw_warnings.arbitration_id == 0x789
        assert msg_gw_warnings.data[0] & 0x01 == 0x01

    def test_tc_002_door_closed_while_vehicle_in_motion(self, can_bus):
        # Send ECM_VehicleData message with non-zero vehicle speed
        msg_ecm_vehicle_data = Message(arbitration_id=0x123, data=b'\x00\x00\x32\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_ecm_vehicle_data)
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with driver door open status set to closed
        msg_bcm_body_status_driver_door = Message(arbitration_id=0x456, data=b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_bcm_body_status_driver_door)
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with passenger door open status set to closed
        msg_bcm_body_status_passenger_door = Message(arbitration_id=0x456, data=b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01')
        can_bus.send(msg_bcm_body_status_passenger_door)
        
        # Collect received messages
        msgs = can_bus.recv(1)
        
        # Assert GW_Warnings message with DoorWarning signal set to 0 (No Warning)
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        msg_gw_warnings = msgs[0]
        assert msg_gw_warnings.arbitration_id == 0x789
        assert msg_gw_warnings.data[0] & 0x01 == 0x00

    def test_tc_003_vehicle_stationary_with_open_door(self, can_bus):
        # Send ECM_VehicleData message with zero vehicle speed
        msg_ecm_vehicle_data = Message(arbitration_id=0x123, data=b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_ecm_vehicle_data)
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with driver door open status set to open
        msg_bcm_body_status = Message(arbitration_id=0x456, data=b'\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_bcm_body_status)
        
        # Collect received messages
        msgs = can_bus.recv(1)
        
        # Assert GW_Warnings message with DoorWarning signal set to 0 (No Warning)
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        msg_gw_warnings = msgs[0]
        assert msg_gw_warnings.arbitration_id == 0x789
        assert msg_gw_warnings.data[0] & 0x01 == 0x00

    def test_tc_004_door_open_threshold_test(self, can_bus):
        # Send ECM_VehicleData message with minimum vehicle speed threshold value
        msg_ecm_vehicle_data = Message(arbitration_id=0x123, data=b'\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_ecm_vehicle_data)
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with driver door open status set to open
        msg_bcm_body_status = Message(arbitration_id=0x456, data=b'\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_bcm_body_status)
        
        # Collect received messages
        msgs = can_bus.recv(1)
        
        # Assert GW_Warnings message with DoorWarning signal set to 1 (Warning)
        assert len(msgs) == 1
        msg_gw_warnings = msgs[0]
        assert msg_gw_warnings.arbitration_id == 0x789
        assert msg_gw_warnings.data[0] & 0x01 == 0x01

    def test_tc_005_door_warning_timing_test(self, can_bus):
        # Send ECM_VehicleData message with non-zero vehicle speed
        msg_ecm_vehicle_data = Message(arbitration_id=0x123, data=b'\x00\x00\x32\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_ecm_vehicle_data)
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with driver door open status set to open
        msg_bcm_body_status = Message(arbitration_id=0x456, data=b'\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_bcm_body_status)
        
        # Collect received messages within the expected timing threshold
        msgs = []
        for _ in range(10):
            msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
            if msg:
                msgs.append(msg[0])
        
        # Assert GW_Warnings message with DoorWarning signal set to 1 (Warning) within the expected timing threshold
        assert len(msgs) > 0
        for msg in msgs:
            assert msg.arbitration_id == 0x789
            assert msg.data[0] & 0x01 == 0x01

    def test_tc_006_door_warning_state_transition_test(self, can_bus):
        # Send ECM_VehicleData message with non-zero vehicle speed
        msg_ecm_vehicle_data = Message(arbitration_id=0x123, data=b'\x00\x00\x32\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_ecm_vehicle_data)
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with driver door open status set to open
        msg_bcm_body_status_open = Message(arbitration_id=0x456, data=b'\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_bcm_body_status_open)
        
        # Collect received messages
        msgs_open = can_bus.recv(1)
        
        # Assert GW_Warnings message with DoorWarning signal set to 1 (Warning) when the door is open
        assert len(msgs_open) == 1
        msg_gw_warnings_open = msgs_open[0]
        assert msg_gw_warnings_open.arbitration_id == 0x789
        assert msg_gw_warnings_open.data[0] & 0x01 == 0x01
        
        # Send BCM_BodyStatus message with driver door open status set to closed
        msg_bcm_body_status_closed = Message(arbitration_id=0x456, data=b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')
        can_bus.send(msg_bcm_body_status_closed)
        
        # Collect received messages
        msgs_closed = can_bus.recv(1)
        
        # Assert GW_Warnings message with DoorWarning signal set to 0 (No Warning) when the door is closed
        assert len(msgs_closed) == 1
        msg_gw_warnings_closed = msgs_closed[0]
        assert msg_gw_warnings_closed.arbitration_id == 0x789
        assert msg_gw_warnings_closed.data[0] & 0x01 == 0x00

user_input.dbc_content:
======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_VehicleData

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_BodyStatus

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_VehicleStatus, GW_Warnings

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: ECM_VehicleData
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x64
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: VehicleSpeed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h
    SIGNAL: EngineRPM
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8000] rpm

MESSAGE: BCM_BodyStatus
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x6E
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: DriverDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: PassengerDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: LeftIndicator
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: RightIndicator
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_VehicleStatus
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0xC8
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GW_SpeedOut
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h
    SIGNAL: AnyDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: TurnActive
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Warnings
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0xD2
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: OverspeedWarning
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: DoorWarning
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=No Warning, 1=Warning

The engine controller shall periodically transmit the current engine rotational speed value to the gateway and instrument cluster ECUs.

--- SIMILAR REQUIREMENTS #3 ---
The gateway shall periodically transmit a vehicle speed value derived from the most recently received engine speed or wheel speed information.

response.test_scenario:
# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Vehicle Speed Transmission to GW

**Description**: Verify vehicle speed is transmitted from ECU to Gateway

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send ECM_Powertrain_Data message', 'signal': 'ECM_Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to 85 km/h'}
- {'action': 'GW receives message', 'signal': 'ECM_Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Verify GW receives correct vehicle speed value'}

**Expected Result**: Vehicle speed is transmitted correctly to GW

## TC_002: Engine Speed Transmission to GW

**Description**: Verify engine speed is transmitted from ECU to Gateway

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send ECM_Powertrain_Data message', 'signal': 'ECM_Engine_Speed', 'value': 1500, 'description': 'Set engine speed to 1500 rpm'}
- {'action': 'GW receives message', 'signal': 'ECM_Engine_Speed', 'value': 1500, 'description': 'Verify GW receives correct engine speed value'}

**Expected Result**: Engine speed is transmitted correctly to GW

## TC_003: Vehicle Speed Edge Case (Max Value)

**Description**: Verify vehicle speed transmission when at maximum allowed value

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send ECM_Powertrain_Data message', 'signal': 'ECM_Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 260, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to maximum allowed value'}
- {'action': 'GW receives message', 'signal': 'ECM_Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 260, 'description': 'Verify GW receives correct vehicle speed value at max limit'}

**Expected Result**: Vehicle speed transmission is successful even at maximum allowed value

## TC_004: Engine Speed Edge Case (Min Value)

**Description**: Verify engine speed transmission when at minimum allowed value

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send ECM_Powertrain_Data message', 'signal': 'ECM_Engine_Speed', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set engine speed to minimum allowed value'}
- {'action': 'GW receives message', 'signal': 'ECM_Engine_Speed', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify GW receives correct engine speed value at min limit'}

**Expected Result**: Engine speed transmission is successful even at minimum allowed value

## TC_005: CAN Bus Disconnection

**Description**: Verify vehicle speed and engine speed transmission when CAN bus is disconnected

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Disconnect CAN bus'}
- {'action': 'Send ECM_Powertrain_Data message', 'signal': 'ECM_Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Attempt to send vehicle speed value while CAN bus is disconnected'}

**Expected Result**: Error occurs when attempting to transmit values over disconnected CAN bus

## TC_006: ECU Initialization Failure

**Description**: Verify vehicle speed and engine speed transmission when ECU initialization fails

**Preconditions**: ['CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Simulate ECU initialization failure'}
- {'action': 'Send ECM_Powertrain_Data message', 'signal': 'ECM_Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Attempt to send vehicle speed value after ECU initialization fails'}

**Expected Result**: Error occurs when attempting to transmit values after ECU initialization failure

test_cases.description: The gateway shall derive a consolidated door status based on the evaluated individual door indications.

user_input.requirement: The gateway shall derive a consolidated door status based on the evaluated individual door indications.
user_input.dbc_content:
======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_VehicleData

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_BodyStatus

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_VehicleStatus, GW_Warnings

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: ECM_VehicleData
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x64
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: VehicleSpeed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h
    SIGNAL: EngineRPM
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8000] rpm

MESSAGE: BCM_BodyStatus
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x6E
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: DriverDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: PassengerDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: LeftIndicator
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: RightIndicator
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_VehicleStatus
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0xC8
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GW_SpeedOut
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h
    SIGNAL: AnyDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: TurnActive
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Warnings
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0xD2
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: OverspeedWarning
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: DoorWarning
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=No Warning, 1=Warning

ground_truth.CAPL_code: variables { msTimer gwTxTimer; int driverDoor; int passengerDoor; int anyDoorOpen; } on start { write("GW Simulation Started - Door Consolidation Active"); setTimer(gwTxTimer, 100); } on message BCM_BodyStatus { driverDoor = this.DriverDoorOpen; passengerDoor = this.PassengerDoorOpen; if ((driverDoor == 1) || (passengerDoor == 1)) { anyDoorOpen = 1; } else { anyDoorOpen = 0; } write("BCM Received -> DriverDoor=%d PassengerDoor=%d => AnyDoorOpen=%d", driverDoor, passengerDoor, anyDoorOpen); } on timer gwTxTimer { message GW_VehicleStatus gwMsg; gwMsg.AnyDoorOpen = anyDoorOpen; gwMsg.GW_SpeedOut = 0; gwMsg.TurnActive = 0; output(gwMsg); setTimer(gwTxTimer, 100); }
ground_truth.Python_code: # ============================================================ # ECU Gateway – GW-REQ-04 Derived Door Consolidation # ============================================================ import can import pytest import time MSG_ID_GW_VEHICLE_STATUS = 0xC8 @pytest.fixture(scope="module") def can_bus(): bus = can.Bus(bustype="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000) yield bus bus.shutdown() def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec): msgs = [] end_time = time.time() + duration_sec while time.time() < end_time: msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.5) if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id: msgs.append(msg) return msgs def decode_any_door_open(msg): return (msg.data[2] >> 0) & 0x01 def test_tc_gw_door_con_001_all_closed(can_bus): msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MSG_ID_GW_VEHICLE_STATUS, 2) assert msgs for msg in msgs: assert decode_any_door_open(msg) == 0 def test_tc_gw_door_con_002_driver_open(can_bus): msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MSG_ID_GW_VEHICLE_STATUS, 2) assert msgs for msg in msgs: assert decode_any_door_open(msg) == 1 def test_tc_gw_door_con_003_passenger_open(can_bus): msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MSG_ID_GW_VEHICLE_STATUS, 2) assert msgs for msg in msgs: assert decode_any_door_open(msg) == 1 def test_tc_gw_door_con_004_both_open(can_bus): msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MSG_ID_GW_VEHICLE_STATUS, 2) assert msgs for msg in msgs: assert decode_any_door_open(msg) == 1 def test_tc_gw_door_con_005_transition_set(can_bus): msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MSG_ID_GW_VEHICLE_STATUS, 3) assert msgs states = [decode_any_door_open(m) for m in msgs] assert 0 in states and 1 in states assert states[-1] == 1 def test_tc_gw_door_con_006_transition_clear(can_bus): msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MSG_ID_GW_VEHICLE_STATUS, 3) assert msgs states = [decode_any_door_open(m) for m in msgs] assert states[-1] == 0 def test_tc_gw_door_con_007_partial_close(can_bus): msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MSG_ID_GW_VEHICLE_STATUS, 3) assert msgs for msg in msgs: assert decode_any_door_open(msg) == 1
ground_truth.test_scenario: Test Case ID: TC-GW-DOOR-CON-001 Requirement ID: GW-REQ-04 Requirement: The gateway shall derive a consolidated door status based on the evaluated individual door indications. Test Objective: Verify consolidated door status when all monitored doors are closed. Preconditions: Vehicle ignition ON; BCM transmitting BCM_BodyStatus; CAN monitoring tool connected and configured for message ID 0xC8 (200 decimal for GW_VehicleStatus). Test Steps: 1. Start the vehicle ignition. 2. Ensure DriverDoorOpen = 0 and PassengerDoorOpen = 0. 3. Monitor the CAN bus for GW_VehicleStatus message. 4. Extract the AnyDoorOpen signal from bit 16. Expected Result: AnyDoorOpen signal value is 0, indicating all doors are closed. Postconditions: Reset vehicle to ignition OFF; disconnect CAN monitoring tool. Test Type: Functional. Test Case ID: TC-GW-DOOR-CON-002 Requirement ID: GW-REQ-04 Requirement: Same as above. Test Objective: Verify consolidated door status when driver door is open. Preconditions: Same as TC-GW-DOOR-CON-001. Test Steps: 1. Set DriverDoorOpen = 1 and PassengerDoorOpen = 0. 2. Monitor GW_VehicleStatus. 3. Extract AnyDoorOpen. Expected Result: AnyDoorOpen = 1 indicating at least one door open. Postconditions: Same. Test Type: Functional. Test Case ID: TC-GW-DOOR-CON-003 Requirement ID: GW-REQ-04 Requirement: Same. Test Objective: Verify consolidated door status when passenger door is open. Preconditions: Same. Test Steps: 1. Set DriverDoorOpen = 0 and PassengerDoorOpen = 1. 2. Monitor GW_VehicleStatus. Expected Result: AnyDoorOpen = 1. Postconditions: Same. Test Type: Functional. Test Case ID: TC-GW-DOOR-CON-004 Requirement ID: GW-REQ-04 Requirement: Same. Test Objective: Verify consolidated door status when both doors are open. Preconditions: Same. Test Steps: 1. Set DriverDoorOpen = 1 and PassengerDoorOpen = 1. 2. Monitor GW_VehicleStatus. Expected Result: AnyDoorOpen = 1. Postconditions: Same. Test Type: Functional. Test Case ID: TC-GW-DOOR-CON-005 Requirement ID: GW-REQ-04 Requirement: Same. Test Objective: Verify consolidated door status updates when any door transitions from closed to open. Preconditions: Same. Test Steps: 1. Ensure both doors are closed. 2. Open driver door. 3. Monitor the next GW_VehicleStatus message. Expected Result: AnyDoorOpen transitions from 0 to 1 immediately. Postconditions: Same. Test Type: Functional. Test Case ID: TC-GW-DOOR-CON-006 Requirement ID: GW-REQ-04 Requirement: Same. Test Objective: Verify consolidated door status clears when all doors are closed again. Preconditions: Same. Test Steps: 1. Ensure at least one door is open. 2. Close all doors. 3. Monitor GW_VehicleStatus. Expected Result: AnyDoorOpen returns to 0. Postconditions: Same. Test Type: Functional. Test Case ID: TC-GW-DOOR-CON-007 Requirement ID: GW-REQ-04 Requirement: Same. Test Objective: Verify consolidated door status remains asserted if one door remains open. Preconditions: Same. Test Steps: 1. Open both doors. 2. Close only one door. 3. Monitor GW_VehicleStatus. Expected Result: AnyDoorOpen remains 1. Postconditions: Same. Test Type: Robustness.
