user_input.dbc_content:
======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: GatewayECU
  TRANSMITS: Gateway_Status, Gateway_Warnings, Gateway_Response

ECU: EngineECU
  TRANSMITS: Engine_Data

ECU: ADAS_ECU
  TRANSMITS: Vehicle_Dynamics

ECU: BodyECU
  TRANSMITS: Body_Status

ECU: ClusterECU
  TRANSMITS: Gateway_Command

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: Gateway_Status
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x2BC
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GatewayMode
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 7] 
    SIGNAL: NetworkHealth
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 
    SIGNAL: AliveCounter
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 15] 

MESSAGE: Engine_Data
  Transmitter: EngineECU
  CAN ID: 0x2C6
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: EngineRPM
      Data Type: float (scale=0.25, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 16000] rpm
    SIGNAL: EngineTemp
      Data Type: float (scale=1, offset=-40)
      Range: [-40 to 215] degC

MESSAGE: Vehicle_Dynamics
  Transmitter: ADAS_ECU
  CAN ID: 0x2D0
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: VehicleSpeed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.01, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h
    SIGNAL: YawRate
      Data Type: float (scale=0.01, offset=0)
      Range: [-327.68 to 327.67] deg/s

MESSAGE: Body_Status
  Transmitter: BodyECU
  CAN ID: 0x2DA
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: DoorFL
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: DoorFR
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: DoorRL
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: DoorRR
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: LightSwitch
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 

MESSAGE: Gateway_Warnings
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x2E4
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: OverspeedWarn
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: DoorOpenWarn
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: HighTempWarn
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: FaultPresent
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: Gateway_Command
  Transmitter: ClusterECU
  CAN ID: 0x2EE
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: ResetGateway
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: RequestStatus
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: Gateway_Response
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x2F8
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: ResetAck
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: StatusValid
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: ErrorCode
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255]

response.python_code:
Here is the Python code that corresponds to the provided CAPL script and test cases:

import time
from capl import CaplSimulator, CaplMessage

# Define constants
GW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW_ID = 0x1234
FL_DOOR_STATUS = 0x0001
BK_DOOR_STATUS = 0x0002

# Define messages
class Gateway_Status(CaplMessage):
    def __init__(self, doorFL=None, doorBR=None, doorFR=None, doorRR=None):
        super().__init__()
        self.doorFL = doorFL
        self.doorBR = doorBR
        self.doorFR = doorFR
        self.doorRR = doorRR

# Define test cases
def test_tc_gw_door_001_all_closed(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, GW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) == 0

def test_tc_gw_door_002_front_left_open(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, GW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_gws_any_door_open(msg) == 1

def test_tc_gw_door_003_front_right_open(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, GW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_gws_any_door_open(msg) == 1

def test_tc_gw_door_004_back_left_open(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, GW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_gws_any_door_open(msg) == 1

def test_tc_gw_door_005_back_right_open(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, GW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_gws_any_door_open(msg) == 1

# Define helper function to simulate door status messages
def send_door_status_message(door_status):
    gateway_status = Gateway_Status(doorFL=door_status, doorBR=None, doorFR=None, doorRR=None)
    can_bus.send_message(gateway_status)

# Define helper function to decode GWS_ANY_DOOR_OPEN message
def decode_gws_any_door_open(msg):
    return msg.doorFL == 1

# Run test cases
if __name__ == "__main__":
    capl_simulator = CaplSimulator()
    capl_simulator.start()

    # Test case 1: All doors closed
    start_time = time.time()
    send_door_status_message(0)
    end_time = time.time()
    assert (end_time - start_time) < 100

    # Test case 2: Front left door open
    start_time = time.time()
    send_door_status_message(1)
    end_time = time.time()
    assert (end_time - start_time) < 100

    # Test case 3: Front right door open
    start_time = time.time()
    send_door_status_message(2)
    end_time = time.time()
    assert (end_time - start_time) < 100

    # Test case 4: Back left door open
    start_time = time.time()
    send_door_status_message(3)
    end_time = time.time()
    assert (end_time - start_time) < 100

    # Test case 5: Back right door open
    start_time = time.time()
    send_door_status_message(4)
    end_time = time.time()
    assert (end_time - start_time) < 100

    capl_simulator.stop()
This code defines the necessary classes and functions to simulate the behavior of the Gateway ECU, including sending door status messages and decoding GWS_ANY_DOOR_OPEN messages. It then runs a series of test cases to verify that the expected behavior is observed when different doors are opened or closed.

# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Front Door Open Test

**Description**: Test that the front door status is sent correctly when it is open

**Preconditions**: ['ECM initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send DoorStatusMessage', 'signal': 'FR_Door_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Send door status message with front door open'}
- {'action': 'Wait for 100ms', 'signal': '', 'value': '', 'description': 'Wait for the CAN bus to process the message'}

**Expected Result**: The 'FR_Door_Status' signal should be received with value 1

## TC_002: Front Door Closed Test

**Description**: Test that the front door status is sent correctly when it is closed

**Preconditions**: ['ECM initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send DoorStatusMessage', 'signal': 'FR_Door_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Send door status message with front door closed'}
- {'action': 'Wait for 100ms', 'signal': '', 'value': '', 'description': 'Wait for the CAN bus to process the message'}

**Expected Result**: The 'FR_Door_Status' signal should be received with value 0

## TC_003: Back Door Open Test

**Description**: Test that the back door status is sent correctly when it is open

**Preconditions**: ['ECM initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send DoorStatusMessage', 'signal': 'BK_Door_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Send door status message with back door open'}
- {'action': 'Wait for 100ms', 'signal': '', 'value': '', 'description': 'Wait for the CAN bus to process the message'}

**Expected Result**: The 'BK_Door_Status' signal should be received with value 1

## TC_004: Back Door Closed Test

**Description**: Test that the back door status is sent correctly when it is closed

**Preconditions**: ['ECM initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send DoorStatusMessage', 'signal': 'BK_Door_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Send door status message with back door closed'}
- {'action': 'Wait for 100ms', 'signal': '', 'value': '', 'description': 'Wait for the CAN bus to process the message'}

**Expected Result**: The 'BK_Door_Status' signal should be received with value 0

## TC_005: Door Status Not Sent Test

**Description**: Test that no door status is sent when the CAN bus is not active

**Preconditions**: ['ECM initialized', 'CAN bus inactive']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send DoorStatusMessage', 'signal': '', 'value': '', 'description': 'Attempt to send door status message'}
- {'action': 'Wait for 100ms', 'signal': '', 'value': '', 'description': 'Wait for the CAN bus to process the message'}

**Expected Result**: No 'Door_Status' signal should be received within 100ms

## TC_006: Timing Test

**Description**: Test that the door status messages are sent within a certain time frame

**Preconditions**: ['ECM initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send DoorStatusMessage', 'signal': 'FR_Door_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Send door status message with front door open'}
- {'action': 'Wait for 50ms', 'signal': '', 'value': '', 'description': 'Wait for the CAN bus to process the message'}

**Expected Result**: The 'FR_Door_Status' signal should be received within 100ms

======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_Status_1

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_Vehicle_Speed

ECU: IC
  TRANSMITS: IC_Driver_Inputs

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_Consolidated_Status

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: BCM_Status_1
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x100
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Hazard_Light_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Headlight_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 
    SIGNAL: Door_Lock_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Trunk_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Interior_Light_Level
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 100] %

MESSAGE: ECM_Vehicle_Speed
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x200
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Vehicle_Speed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h

MESSAGE: IC_Driver_Inputs
  Transmitter: IC
  CAN ID: 0x300
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Horn_Switch_Pressed
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: High_Beam_Switch_Requested
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Consolidated_Status
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0x400
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg
      Data Type: float (scale=0.5, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h
    SIGNAL: GWS_Any_Door_Open
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: GWS_Horn_Active
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1]

response.test_scenario:
# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Interior Light Level Test

**Description**: Test that interior light level is set correctly when ECU initializes

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send SpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to 85 km/h'}
- {'action': 'Wait for InteriorLightLevelMessage', 'signal': 'InteriorLightLevel', 'value': 50, 'description': 'Wait for interior light level message'}

**Expected Result**: Interior light level is set to 50%

## TC_002: Horn Switch Test

**Description**: Test that horn switch is detected correctly when pressed

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send HornSwitchPressedMessage', 'signal': 'HornSwitchPressed', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Press horn switch'}
- {'action': 'Wait for HornSwitchDetectedMessage', 'signal': 'HornSwitchDetected', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Wait for horn switch detected message'}

**Expected Result**: Horn switch is detected correctly

## TC_003: High Beam Switch Test

**Description**: Test that high beam switch is detected correctly when requested

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send HighBeamSwitchRequestedMessage', 'signal': 'HighBeamSwitchRequested', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Request high beam switch'}
- {'action': 'Wait for HighBeamSwitchDetectedMessage', 'signal': 'HighBeamSwitchDetected', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Wait for high beam switch detected message'}

**Expected Result**: High beam switch is detected correctly

## TC_004: Edge Case: Low Vehicle Speed Test

**Description**: Test that interior light level is set correctly when vehicle speed is low

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send SpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Speed', 'value': 10, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to 10 km/h'}
- {'action': 'Wait for InteriorLightLevelMessage', 'signal': 'InteriorLightLevel', 'value': 20, 'description': 'Wait for interior light level message'}

**Expected Result**: Interior light level is set to 20%

## TC_005: Timing Test: Delayed Interior Light Level Message Test

**Description**: Test that interior light level message is delayed correctly

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send SpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to 85 km/h'}
- {'action': 'Wait for DelayedInteriorLightLevelMessage', 'signal': 'DelayedInteriorLightLevel', 'value': 50, 'description': 'Wait for delayed interior light level message'}

**Expected Result**: Interior light level message is delayed correctly

## TC_006: State Transition Test: Horn Switch Pressed to Off Test

**Description**: Test that horn switch state transitions correctly when pressed and released

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send HornSwitchPressedMessage', 'signal': 'HornSwitchPressed', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Press horn switch'}
- {'action': 'Wait for HornSwitchDetectedMessage', 'signal': 'HornSwitchDetected', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Wait for horn switch detected message'}

**Expected Result**: Horn switch state transitions correctly when pressed and released

test_cases.description: The system shall adjust Interior Light Level automatically when transitioning from day mode to night mode based on Headlight Status feedback.

user_input.requirement: The system shall adjust Interior Light Level automatically when transitioning from day mode to night mode based on Headlight Status feedback.
user_input.dbc_content:
======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_Status_1

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_Vehicle_Speed

ECU: IC
  TRANSMITS: IC_Driver_Inputs

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_Consolidated_Status

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: BCM_Status_1
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x100
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Hazard_Light_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Headlight_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 
    SIGNAL: Door_Lock_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Trunk_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Interior_Light_Level
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 100] %

MESSAGE: ECM_Vehicle_Speed
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x200
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Vehicle_Speed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h

MESSAGE: IC_Driver_Inputs
  Transmitter: IC
  CAN ID: 0x300
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Horn_Switch_Pressed
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: High_Beam_Switch_Requested
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Consolidated_Status
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0x400
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg
      Data Type: float (scale=0.5, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h
    SIGNAL: GWS_Any_Door_Open
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: GWS_Horn_Active
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1]

response.python_code:
Here is the code that meets the specifications:
import time
from capl import *

# Define variables
cCycleTime = 100  # cycle time in milliseconds

# Initialize variables on start
def on_start():
    log("Simulation started.")
    msgECMInteriorLight.Interior_Light_Level = 0.0
    setTimer(tmECMInteriorLight, cCycleTime)

# Cyclic transmission (timer)
def on_timer(tmECMInteriorLight):
    output(msgECMInteriorLight)
    setTimer(tmECMInteriorLight, cCycleTime)

# Test functions
def test_tc_hl_001_transition(can_bus):
    msg = wait_for_next_bcm_message(can_bus)
    assert get_headlight_status(msg.data) == 3

def test_tc_hb_001_transition_off_to_on(can_bus):
    msg = wait_for_next_ic_frame(can_bus)
    assert get_high_beam_status(msg.data) == 1

# Main function
def main():
    can_bus = create_can_bus()
    start_simulation(can_bus)

# Run the simulation
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
Note that I've assumed that the `capl` library is installed and available. Also, I've used the `create_can_bus()` function to create a CAN bus object, which is not shown in the original specification. You may need to modify this line to match your specific use case.

Additionally, I've defined two test functions: `test_tc_hl_001_transition` and `test_tc_hb_001_transition_off_to_on`. These tests check the transition from day mode to night mode based on Headlight Status feedback. The `get_headlight_status()` and `get_high_beam_status()` functions are not shown in the original specification, so I've assumed they exist elsewhere in your codebase.

Finally, I've defined a main function that creates a CAN bus object and starts the simulation using the `start_simulation()` function. This is not shown in the original specification, but it's likely that this function is part of the `capl` library or another custom implementation.
response.test_scenario:
# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Normal Operation with Interior Light Level

**Description**: Verify interior light level is set correctly when ECU initializes and CAN bus is active

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send SpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to 85 km/h'}
- {'action': 'Get InteriorLightLevel', 'signal': 'Interior_Light_Level', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Check interior light level is initially set to 0%'}

**Expected Result**: Interior light level should be set to 0%

## TC_002: Error Condition: Interior Light Level Not Set

**Description**: Verify error message is sent when interior light level is not set

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send SpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to 85 km/h'}
- {'action': 'Get InteriorLightLevel', 'signal': 'Interior_Light_Level', 'value': None, 'description': 'Check interior light level is not set'}

**Expected Result**: Error message should be sent with interior light level not set

## TC_003: Edge Case: Interior Light Level Set to Maximum Value

**Description**: Verify error message is sent when interior light level is set to maximum value

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send SpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to 85 km/h'}
- {'action': 'Get InteriorLightLevel', 'signal': 'Interior_Light_Level', 'value': 100, 'description': 'Check interior light level is set to maximum value (100%)'}

**Expected Result**: Error message should be sent with interior light level set to maximum value

## TC_004: Timing Test: Interior Light Level Update Interval

**Description**: Verify interior light level is updated correctly at the specified interval

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send SpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to 85 km/h'}
- {'action': 'Get InteriorLightLevel', 'signal': 'Interior_Light_Level', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Check interior light level is initially set to 0%'}

**Expected Result**: Interior light level should be updated correctly at the specified interval

## TC_005: State Transition Test: Interior Light Level Set and Cleared

**Description**: Verify interior light level is set and cleared correctly when ECU initializes and CAN bus is active

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send SpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Set vehicle speed to 85 km/h'}
- {'action': 'Get InteriorLightLevel', 'signal': 'Interior_Light_Level', 'value': 100, 'description': 'Check interior light level is set to maximum value (100%)'}

**Expected Result**: Interior light level should be cleared correctly when ECU initializes and CAN bus is active

======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: GatewayECU
  TRANSMITS: GW_OperationalPresence, GW_IgnitionStatus, GW_VehicleSpeed, GW_BatteryVoltage, GW_SoftwareVersion, GW_DoorStatus, GW_ExteriorLighting, GW_BrakeWarning, GW_ReverseGear, GW_FaultStatus, GW_SafetyMessage

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: GW_OperationalPresence
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x100
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GW_AliveCounter
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255] 
    SIGNAL: GW_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_IgnitionStatus
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x101
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: IgnitionState
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 

MESSAGE: GW_VehicleSpeed
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x102
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: VehicleSpeed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.01, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h

MESSAGE: GW_BatteryVoltage
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x103
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: BatteryVoltage
      Data Type: float (scale=0.01, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 20] V

MESSAGE: GW_SoftwareVersion
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x104
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: SW_Major
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255] 
    SIGNAL: SW_Minor
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255] 
    SIGNAL: SW_Patch
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255] 

MESSAGE: GW_DoorStatus
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x10E
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Door_FL
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Door_FR
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Door_RL
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Door_RR
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: AnyDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_ExteriorLighting
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x10F
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: HeadlampStatus
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: TailLampStatus
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: HazardStatus
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_BrakeWarning
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x110
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: BrakeWarningActive
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_ReverseGear
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x111
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: ReverseActive
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_FaultStatus
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x118
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: DoorSignalTimeout
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: SpeedSignalTimeout
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_SafetyMessage
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x119
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: SafetyData
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 4294967295] 
    SIGNAL: MsgCounter
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 15] 
    SIGNAL: CRC
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255]

response.test_scenario:
# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Test Vehicle Speed with Normal Operation

**Description**: Verify that the vehicle speed is correctly reported when the ECU sends a normal operation message

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send VehicleSpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Send a normal operation message with vehicle speed'}
- {'action': 'Wait for Response', 'signal': 'Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Wait for the response from the ECU'}

**Expected Result**: The vehicle speed is correctly reported as 85 km/h

## TC_002: Test Vehicle Speed with Error Condition

**Description**: Verify that an error condition is reported when the ECU sends a message with invalid data

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send VehicleSpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 999, 'description': 'Send a message with invalid vehicle speed'}
- {'action': 'Wait for Response', 'signal': 'Error_Code', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Wait for the response from the ECU'}

**Expected Result**: An error code is reported as 1

## TC_003: Test Vehicle Speed with Boundary Value

**Description**: Verify that the vehicle speed is correctly reported when the ECU sends a message with boundary value

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send VehicleSpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Send a message with boundary value'}
- {'action': 'Wait for Response', 'signal': 'Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Wait for the response from the ECU'}

**Expected Result**: The vehicle speed is correctly reported as 0 km/h

## TC_004: Test Timing/Sequence with Normal Operation

**Description**: Verify that the timing and sequence of messages are correct when the ECU sends a normal operation message

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send VehicleSpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Send a normal operation message'}
- {'action': 'Wait for Response', 'signal': 'Vehicle_Speed_Avg', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Wait for the response from the ECU'}

**Expected Result**: The timing and sequence of messages are correct

## TC_005: Test State Transition with Error Condition

**Description**: Verify that a state transition occurs when an error condition is reported

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send VehicleSpeedMessage', 'signal': 'Vehicle_Speed', 'value': 999, 'description': 'Send a message with invalid vehicle speed'}
- {'action': 'Wait for Response', 'signal': 'Error_Code', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Wait for the response from the ECU'}

**Expected Result**: A state transition occurs and an error code is reported

test_cases.description: The gateway shall immediately forward the brake warning signal when it becomes active.

user_input.requirement: The gateway shall immediately forward the brake warning signal when it becomes active.
user_input.dbc_content:
======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: GatewayECU
  TRANSMITS: GW_OperationalPresence, GW_IgnitionStatus, GW_VehicleSpeed, GW_BatteryVoltage, GW_SoftwareVersion, GW_DoorStatus, GW_ExteriorLighting, GW_BrakeWarning, GW_ReverseGear, GW_FaultStatus, GW_SafetyMessage

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: GW_OperationalPresence
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x100
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GW_AliveCounter
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255] 
    SIGNAL: GW_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_IgnitionStatus
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x101
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: IgnitionState
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 

MESSAGE: GW_VehicleSpeed
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x102
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: VehicleSpeed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.01, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 250] km/h

MESSAGE: GW_BatteryVoltage
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x103
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: BatteryVoltage
      Data Type: float (scale=0.01, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 20] V

MESSAGE: GW_SoftwareVersion
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x104
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: SW_Major
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255] 
    SIGNAL: SW_Minor
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255] 
    SIGNAL: SW_Patch
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255] 

MESSAGE: GW_DoorStatus
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x10E
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Door_FL
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Door_FR
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Door_RL
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Door_RR
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: AnyDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_ExteriorLighting
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x10F
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: HeadlampStatus
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: TailLampStatus
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: HazardStatus
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_BrakeWarning
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x110
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: BrakeWarningActive
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_ReverseGear
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x111
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:

test_cases.description: The system shall adjust Interior Light Level automatically when transitioning from day mode to night mode based on Headlight Status feedback.

user_input.requirement: The system shall adjust Interior Light Level automatically when transitioning from day mode to night mode based on Headlight Status feedback.
user_input.dbc_content:
======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_Status_1

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_Vehicle_Speed

ECU: IC
  TRANSMITS: IC_Driver_Inputs

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_Consolidated_Status

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: BCM_Status_1
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x100
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Hazard_Light_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Headlight_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 
    SIGNAL: Door_Lock_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Trunk_Status
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Interior_Light_Level
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 100] %

MESSAGE: ECM_Vehicle_Speed
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0x200
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Vehicle_Speed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h

MESSAGE: IC_Driver_Inputs
  Transmitter: IC
  CAN ID: 0x300
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: Horn_Switch_Pressed
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: High_Beam_Switch_Requested
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Consolidated_Status
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0x400
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GWS_Vehicle_Speed_Avg
      Data Type: float (scale=0.5, offset=0)

Here is the complete Python code based on the provided specifications:
import time
from capl import CAPL

# Define a function to wait for the next BCM message
def wait_for_next_bcm_message(can_bus):
    while True:
        msg = can_bus.recv()
        if msg.id == 0x123:  # Replace with the actual BCM message ID
            return msg

# Define a function to get the Headlight Status from a message
def get_headlight_status(data):
    # Assuming the Headlight Status is stored in byte 2 of the message data
    return data[2]

# Define a function to wait for the next IC frame
def wait_for_next_ic_frame(can_bus):
    while True:
        msg = can_bus.recv()
        if msg.id == 0x456:  # Replace with the actual IC message ID
            return msg

# Define a function to get the High Beam Status from a message
def get_high_beam_status(data):
    # Assuming the High Beam Status is stored in byte 3 of the message data
    return data[3]

# Test cases
def test_tc_gw_door_001_all_closed(can_bus):
    msg = wait_for_next_bcm_message(can_bus)
    assert get_headlight_status(msg.data) == 0

def test_tc_hl_003_transition(can_bus):
    msg = wait_for_next_bcm_message(can_bus)
    assert get_headlight_status(msg.data) == 3

def test_tc_hb_003_transition_off_to_on(can_bus):
    msg = wait_for_next_ic_frame(can_bus)
    assert get_high_beam_status(msg.data) == 1

# Main function
def main():
    can_bus = CAPL('CAN_BUS')  # Replace with the actual CAN bus name
    test_tc_gw_door_001_all_closed(can_bus)
    test_tc_hl_003_transition(can_bus)
    test_tc_hb_003_transition_off_to_on(can_bus)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
Note that this code assumes you have a CAPL library installed and configured properly. You may need to modify the code to match your specific setup.

Also, I've only included the test cases mentioned in the specification. If you want to add more test cases or modify these ones, please let me know!
response.test_scenario:
# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Headlight Status Update

**Description**: Verify Headlight status is updated correctly

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM Status Message', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 2, 'description': "Update Headlight status to 'High Beams On'"}

**Expected Result**: ECM, IC and GW should receive the updated Headlight status

## TC_002: Invalid Speed Message

**Description**: Verify invalid speed message is handled correctly

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Invalid Speed Message', 'signal': 'Speed', 'value': 301, 'description': 'Send speed message with invalid value'}

**Expected Result**: ECM should ignore the invalid speed message and maintain previous speed value

## TC_003: Interior Light Level Update

**Description**: Verify Interior Light level is updated correctly

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM Status Message', 'signal': 'Interior_Light_Level', 'value': 50, 'description': "Update Interior Light level to '50%'"}

**Expected Result**: IC and GW should receive the updated Interior Light level

## TC_004: CAN Bus Error

**Description**: Verify CAN bus error is handled correctly

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Simulate CAN Bus Error', 'signal': '', 'value': '', 'description': 'Simulate CAN bus error'}

**Expected Result**: ECU should recover from the CAN bus error and continue normal operation

## TC_005: Headlight Status Transition

**Description**: Verify Headlight status transition is handled correctly

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM Status Message', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': "Update Headlight status to 'High Beams Off'"}
- {'action': 'Send BCM Status Message', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 2, 'description': "Update Headlight status to 'High Beams On'"}

**Expected Result**: ECM, IC and GW should receive the updated Headlight status

## TC_006: Speed Message Timing

**Description**: Verify speed message timing is handled correctly

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Speed Message at 100ms interval', 'signal': 'Speed', 'value': 85, 'description': 'Send speed message at 100ms interval'}

**Expected Result**: ECM should receive the speed messages at regular intervals

response.python_code:
import can
import pytest
import time

BCM_Status_1_ID = 0x100  # 256 decimal

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    """Collect CAN messages for arbitration_id over duration_sec."""
    messages = []
    start_time = time.time()
    while time.time() - start_time < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            messages.append(msg)
    return messages

def decode_headlight_status(msg):
    """Extract Headlight_Status from CAN message. start_bit=1, length=2, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 1) & 0x3

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(
        interface="socketcan",   # vector / pcan configurable
        channel="can0",
        bitrate=500000
    )
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def test_tc_hl_001_positive(can_bus):
    messages = collect_messages(can_bus, BCM_Status_1_ID, 5)
    assert len(messages) > 0
    msg = messages[0]
    assert decode_headlight_status(msg) == 1

def test_tc_hl_002_negative(can_bus):
    messages = collect_messages(can_bus, BCM_Status_1_ID, 5)
    assert len(messages) > 0
    msg = messages[0]
    assert decode_headlight_status(msg) != 0

def test_tc_hl_003_transition(can_bus):
    messages = collect_messages(can_bus, BCM_Status_1_ID, 10)
    assert len(messages) > 2
    msg1 = messages[0]
    msg2 = messages[1]
    assert decode_headlight_status(msg1) == 0
    assert decode_headlight_status(msg2) == 1

# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Positive Test Case - Headlight On

**Description**: Verify that the system reflects the headlight status when it is active.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Signal Value', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Activate the headlight signal'}
- {'action': 'Verify Indicator Status', 'signal': 'Gateway_Indicator', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Check that the gateway indicator is active'}

**Expected Result**: The system should reflect the headlight status in the vehicle user interface or gateway indicators.

## TC_002: Negative Test Case - Headlight Off

**Description**: Verify that the system does not reflect the headlight status when it is inactive.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Signal Value', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Deactivate the headlight signal'}
- {'action': 'Verify Indicator Status', 'signal': 'Gateway_Indicator', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Check that the gateway indicator is inactive'}

**Expected Result**: The system should not reflect the headlight status in the vehicle user interface or gateway indicators.

## TC_003: Edge Case Test - Headlight Status at Boundary Value

**Description**: Verify that the system reflects the headlight status when it is at the boundary value.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Signal Value', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Activate the headlight signal'}
- {'action': 'Verify Indicator Status', 'signal': 'Gateway_Indicator', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Check that the gateway indicator is active'}

**Expected Result**: The system should reflect the headlight status in the vehicle user interface or gateway indicators.

## TC_004: Timing Test Case - Headlight Status Update

**Description**: Verify that the system updates the headlight status within the specified cycle time.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Signal Value', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Activate the headlight signal'}
- {'action': 'Wait for Cycle Time', 'time': 100, 'description': 'Wait for the cycle time to pass'}

**Expected Result**: The system should update the headlight status within the specified cycle time.

## TC_005: State Transition Test Case - Headlight Status Change

**Description**: Verify that the system reflects the new headlight status after a state transition.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Signal Value', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Activate the headlight signal'}
- {'action': 'Set Signal Value', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Deactivate the headlight signal'}

**Expected Result**: The system should reflect the new headlight status after a state transition.

## TC_006: Negative Test Case - Invalid Headlight Status Value

**Description**: Verify that the system does not reflect an invalid headlight status value.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Signal Value', 'signal': 'Headlight_Status', 'value': 2, 'description': 'Set an invalid headlight status value'}
- {'action': 'Verify Indicator Status', 'signal': 'Gateway_Indicator', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Check that the gateway indicator is inactive'}

**Expected Result**: The system should not reflect an invalid headlight status value.

response.python_code:
import can
import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID = 0x100

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(
        interface="socketcan",
        channel="can0",
        bitrate=500000
    )
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def decode_headlight_status(msg):
    """Extract Headlight_Status from CAN message. start_bit=1, length=2, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 1) & 0x3

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    """Collect CAN messages for arbitration_id over duration_sec."""
    messages = []
    start_time = time.time()
    while time.time() - start_time < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            messages.append(msg)
    return messages

def test_tc_day_mode_headlight_on(can_bus):

    msg = messages[0]
    decoded_status = decode_headlight_status(msg)
    assert decoded_status == 0

import can
import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID = 0x2BC

def decode_gatewaymode(msg):
    """Extract GatewayMode from CAN message. start_bit=0, length=3, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x7

def decode_networkhealth(msg):
    """Extract NetworkHealth from CAN message. start_bit=3, length=2, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x3

def decode_alivecounter(msg):
    """Extract AliveCounter from CAN message. start_bit=5, length=4, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 5) & 0xF

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(
        interface="socketcan",   # vector / pcan configurable
        channel="can0",
        bitrate=500000
    )
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    """Collect CAN messages for arbitration_id over duration_sec."""
    messages = []
    start_time = time.time()
    while time.time() - start_time < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            messages.append(msg)
    return messages

def test_tc_gw_alive_counter_transmission(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for m in msgs:
        assert decode_gatewaymode(m) == 0
        assert decode_networkhealth(m) == 0
        assert decode_alivecounter(m) > 0

def test_tc_gw_multiple_frames_observed(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 1
    for m in msgs

# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Periodic Alive Counter Transmission

**Description**: Test that the gateway transmits its alive counter periodically to the display controller

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set AliveCounterSignal', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Initialize alive counter'}
- {'action': 'Send AliveCounterMessage', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Transmit alive counter to display controller'}

**Expected Result**: AliveCounterSignal = 1 and periodic transmission observed

## TC_002: Multiple Frames Observed

**Description**: Test that multiple frames are transmitted within the cycle time

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set AliveCounterSignal', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 2, 'description': 'Increase alive counter'}
- {'action': 'Send AliveCounterMessage', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Transmit alive counter to display controller'}

**Expected Result**: Multiple frames observed and gear value remains stable

## TC_003: Invalid Cycle Time

**Description**: Test that the gateway does not transmit the alive counter with an invalid cycle time

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set AliveCounterSignal', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 3, 'description': 'Increase alive counter'}
- {'action': 'Send AliveCounterMessage', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': -1, 'description': 'Transmit alive counter to display controller with invalid cycle time'}

**Expected Result**: Error or exception thrown due to invalid cycle time

## TC_004: Display Controller Busy

**Description**: Test that the gateway waits for the display controller to become available before transmitting the alive counter

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set AliveCounterSignal', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 4, 'description': 'Increase alive counter'}
- {'action': 'Send AliveCounterMessage', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Transmit alive counter to display controller when it is available'}

**Expected Result**: AliveCounterSignal = 4 and periodic transmission observed after display controller becomes available

## TC_005: ECU Failure

**Description**: Test that the gateway does not transmit the alive counter when an ECU fails

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Simulate EngineECU failure', 'signal': 'Engine_Data', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Simulate engine ECU failure'}
- {'action': 'Send AliveCounterMessage', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 5, 'description': 'Transmit alive counter to display controller when it is available'}

**Expected Result**: Error or exception thrown due to ECU failure

## TC_006: Cycle Time Exceeded

**Description**: Test that the gateway does not transmit the alive counter more than once within the cycle time

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set AliveCounterSignal', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 6, 'description': 'Increase alive counter'}
- {'action': 'Send AliveCounterMessage', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Transmit alive counter to display controller within cycle time'}

**Expected Result**: AliveCounterSignal = 6 and periodic transmission observed within cycle time

response.test_scenario:
# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Alive Counter Transmission - Normal Operation

**Description**: Verify that the Gateway ECU transmits its internal alive counter to the display controller in normal operation.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Alive Counter', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 100, 'description': 'Set the alive counter to a valid value'}
- {'action': 'Send Gateway_Status Message', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Send the Gateway_Status message with the alive counter value'}

**Expected Result**: The display controller receives the Gateway_Status message with the correct alive counter value.

## TC_002: Alive Counter Transmission - Error Condition (ECU not initialized)

**Description**: Verify that the Gateway ECU does not transmit its internal alive counter to the display controller when the ECU is not initialized.

**Preconditions**: ['CAN bus active', 'ECU not initialized']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Gateway_Status Message', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Attempt to send the Gateway_Status message'}

**Expected Result**: The display controller does not receive any Gateway_Status message.

## TC_003: Alive Counter Transmission - Edge Case (Alive Counter value = 0)

**Description**: Verify that the Gateway ECU transmits its internal alive counter to the display controller when the alive counter value is 0.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Alive Counter', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set the alive counter to 0'}
- {'action': 'Send Gateway_Status Message', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Send the Gateway_Status message with the alive counter value'}

**Expected Result**: The display controller receives the Gateway_Status message with an alive counter value of 0.

## TC_004: Alive Counter Transmission - Timing/Sequence Test (Multiple transmissions)

**Description**: Verify that the Gateway ECU transmits its internal alive counter to the display controller at regular intervals.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Alive Counter', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 100, 'description': 'Set the alive counter to a valid value'}
- {'action': 'Send Gateway_Status Message (multiple times)', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Send multiple Gateway_Status messages with the alive counter value'}

**Expected Result**: The display controller receives multiple Gateway_Status messages with the correct alive counter values at regular intervals.

## TC_005: Alive Counter Transmission - State Transition Test (ECU reset)

**Description**: Verify that the Gateway ECU transmits its internal alive counter to the display controller after an ECU reset.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Reset ECU', 'description': 'Reset the ECU'}
- {'action': 'Set Alive Counter', 'signal': 'AliveCounter', 'value': 100, 'description': 'Set the alive counter to a valid value after reset'}
- {'action': 'Send Gateway_Status Message', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Send the Gateway_Status message with the alive counter value after reset'}

**Expected Result**: The display controller receives the Gateway_Status message with the correct alive counter value after ECU reset.

## TC_006: Alive Counter Transmission - Error Condition (CAN bus not active)

**Description**: Verify that the Gateway ECU does not transmit its internal alive counter to the display controller when the CAN bus is not active.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus not active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Gateway_Status Message', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Attempt to send the Gateway_Status message'}

**Expected Result**: The display controller does not receive any Gateway_Status message.

======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: GatewayECU
  TRANSMITS: Gateway_Status, Gateway_Warnings, Gateway_Response

ECU: EngineECU
  TRANSMITS: Engine_Data

ECU: ADAS_ECU
  TRANSMITS: Vehicle_Dynamics

ECU: BodyECU
  TRANSMITS: Body_Status

ECU: ClusterECU
  TRANSMITS: Gateway_Command

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: Gateway_Status
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x2BC
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GatewayMode
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 7] 
    SIGNAL: NetworkHealth
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 
    SIGNAL: AliveCounter
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 15] 

MESSAGE: Engine_Data
  Transmitter: EngineECU
  CAN ID: 0x2C6
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: EngineRPM
      Data Type: float (scale=0.25, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 16000] rpm
    SIGNAL: EngineTemp
      Data Type: float (scale=1, offset=-40)
      Range: [-40 to 215] degC

MESSAGE: Vehicle_Dynamics
  Transmitter: ADAS_ECU
  CAN ID: 0x2D0
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: VehicleSpeed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.01, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] km/h
    SIGNAL: YawRate
      Data Type: float (scale=0.01, offset=0)
      Range: [-327.68 to 327.67] deg/s

MESSAGE: Body_Status
  Transmitter: BodyECU
  CAN ID: 0x2DA
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: DoorFL
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: DoorFR
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: DoorRL
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: DoorRR
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Closed, 1=Open
    SIGNAL: LightSwitch
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3] 

MESSAGE: Gateway_Warnings
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x2E4
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: OverspeedWarn
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: DoorOpenWarn
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: HighTempWarn
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=Inactive, 1=Active
    SIGNAL: FaultPresent
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: Gateway_Command
  Transmitter: ClusterECU
  CAN ID: 0x2EE
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: ResetGateway
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: RequestStatus
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: Gateway_Response
  Transmitter: GatewayECU
  CAN ID: 0x2F8
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: ResetAck
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: StatusValid
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: ErrorCode
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 255]

response.test_scenario:
# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Valid Gateway Command Sent by ClusterECU

**Description**: ClusterECU sends a valid Gateway_Command message to GatewayECU.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send Gateway_Command message from ClusterECU', 'signal': 'Gateway_Command', 'value': 0, 'description': 'ClusterECU sends a valid Gateway_Command message to GatewayECU.'}
- {'action': 'Verify GatewayECU responds with Gateway_Status', 'signal': 'Gateway_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'GatewayECU should respond with a valid Gateway_Status message within the specified cycle time (100 ms).'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU responds with a valid Gateway_Status message.

## TC_002: Invalid Gateway Command Sent by ClusterECU

**Description**: ClusterECU sends an invalid Gateway_Command message to GatewayECU.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send invalid Gateway_Command message from ClusterECU', 'signal': 'Gateway_Command', 'value': 255, 'description': 'ClusterECU sends an invalid Gateway_Command message to GatewayECU.'}
- {'action': 'Verify GatewayECU responds with error or ignores the message', 'signal': 'Gateway_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'GatewayECU should respond with an error or ignore the invalid message.'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU handles the invalid message correctly.

## TC_003: First Message in Sequence from GatewayECU

**Description**: Verify that GatewayECU responds with a valid Gateway_Status message on the first cycle.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send no messages to GatewayECU for 100 ms', 'signal': '', 'value': 0, 'description': 'No messages are sent to GatewayECU for the first cycle.'}
- {'action': 'Verify GatewayECU responds with a valid Gateway_Status message on the next cycle', 'signal': 'Gateway_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'GatewayECU should respond with a valid Gateway_Status message within the specified cycle time (100 ms).'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU responds with a valid Gateway_Status message on the first cycle.

## TC_004: Last Message in Sequence from GatewayECU

**Description**: Verify that GatewayECU responds with a valid Gateway_Status message on the last cycle.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

import can
import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID = 0x2DA  # Body_Status message ID

def decode_body_status(msg):
    """Extract signals from CAN message."""
    return {
        "DoorFL": (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x1,
        "DoorFR": (msg.data[0] >> 1) & 0x1,
        "DoorRL": (msg.data[0] >> 2) & 0x1,
        "DoorRR": (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x1,
        "LightSwitch": (msg.data[1] >> 0) & 0x3
    }

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(
        interface="socketcan",
        channel="can0",
        bitrate=500000
    )
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    """Collect messages from CAN bus."""
    msgs = []
    start_time = time.time()
    while time.time() - start_time < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def test_tc_gw_door_001_all_closed(can_bus):
    """Test that GatewayECU sends a valid Gateway_Status message with all signals closed."""
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 10)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    msg = msgs[0]
    body_status = decode_body_status(msg.data)
    assert body_status["DoorFL"] == 0
    assert body_status["DoorFR"] == 0
    assert body_status["DoorRL"] == 0
    assert body_status["DoorRR"] == 0
    assert body_status["LightSwitch"] == 0

def test_tc_gw_door_002_all_opened(can_bus):
    """Test that GatewayECU sends a valid Gateway_Status message with all signals open."""
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 10)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    msg = msgs[0]
    body_status = decode_body_status(msg.data)
    assert body_status["DoorFL"] == 1
    assert body_status["DoorFR"] == 1
    assert body_status["DoorRL"] == 1
    assert body_status["DoorRR"] == 1
    assert body_status["LightSwitch"] == 3

def test_tc_gw_door_003_all_closed_opened(can_bus):
    """Test that GatewayECU sends a valid Gateway_Status message with mixed signals."""
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 10)

import can
import pytest
import time

# Constants
BODY_STATUS_ID = 0x2DA  # 730 decimal
DOOR_FL_BITMASK = 0x01
DOOR_FR_BITMASK = 0x02
DOOR_RL_BITMASK = 0x04
DOOR_RR_BITMASK = 0x08
LIGHT_SWITCH_BITMASK = 0xC0

# Session fixture: creates a CAN bus connection to be used in tests
@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(
        interface="socketcan",   # vector / pcan configurable
        channel="can0",
        bitrate=500000
    )
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

# Function to collect messages from the CAN bus
def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    msgs = []
    start_time = time.time()
    while time.time() - start_time < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg is not None and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

# Decode functions: extract signals from CAN message data
def decode_door_fl(msg):
    """Extract DoorFL from CAN message"""
    return (msg.data[0] & DOOR_FL_BITMASK) >> 0

def decode_door_fr(msg):
    """Extract DoorFR from CAN message"""
    return (msg.data[0] & DOOR_FR_BITMASK) >> 1

def decode_door_rl(msg):
    """Extract DoorRL from CAN message"""
    return (msg.data[0] & DOOR_RL_BITMASK) >> 2

def decode_door_rr(msg):
    """Extract DoorRR from CAN message"""
    return (msg.data[0] & DOOR_RR_BITMASK) >> 3

def decode_light_switch(msg):
    """Extract LightSwitch from CAN message"""
    return (msg.data[1] & LIGHT_SWITCH_BITMASK) >> 0

# Test functions: test cases for BodyECU messages
def test_tc_body_001_all_closed(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, BODY_STATUS_ID, 2.0)
    assert all(decode_door_fl(m) == 0 and decode_door_fr(m) == 0 and decode_door_rl(m) == 0 and decode_door_rr(m) == 0 for m in msgs)

def test_tc_body_002_all_opened(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, BODY_STATUS_ID, 2.0)
    assert all(decode_door_fl(m) == 1 and decode_door_fr(m) == 1 and decode_door_rl(m) == 1 and decode_door_rr(m) == 1 for m in msgs)

def test_tc_body_003_doors_closed_light_switch_on(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, BODY_STATUS_ID, 2.0)
    assert all(decode_door_fl(m) == 0 and decode_door_fr(m) == 0 and decode_door_rl(m) == 0 and decode_door_rr(m) == 0 and decode_light_switch(m) == 1 for m in msgs)

def test_tc_body_004_doors_opened_light_switch_off(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, BODY_STATUS_ID, 2.0)
    assert all(decode_door_fl(m) == 1 and decode_door_fr(m) == 1 and decode_door_rl(m) == 1 and decode_door_rr(m) == 1 and decode_light_switch(m) == 0 for m in msgs)

**Description**: Verify overspeed warning remains active if threshold not exceeded

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'GatewayECU sends Gateway_Command with overspeed enable', 'signal': 'OverspeedEnable', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Enable overspeed warning'}

## TC_003: Edge - Overspeed Threshold Boundary Value

response.python_code:
Here is the complete Python code for CAN bus testing using the **python-can** library:
import can
import pytest
import time

GW_STATUS_ID = 0x2BC  # Gateway Status message ID

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(
        interface="socketcan",   # vector / pcan configurable
        channel="can0",
        bitrate=500000
    )
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    messages = []
    start_time = time.time()
    while time.time() - start_time < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            messages.append(msg)
    return messages

def decode_gateway_mode(msg):
    """Extract GatewayMode from CAN message."""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x7

def decode_network_health(msg):
    """Extract NetworkHealth from CAN message."""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x3

def decode_alive_counter(msg):
    """Extract AliveCounter from CAN message."""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 5) & 0xF

def test_tc_gw_status_001_normal_operation(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, GW_STATUS_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for m in msgs:
        assert decode_gateway_mode(m) == 1
        assert decode_network_health(m) == 0
        assert decode_alive_counter(m) == 0

def test_tc_gw_status_002_error_condition_missing_report(can_bus):
    # Simulate CAN bus failure to prevent Gateway_Status message transmission
    can_bus.shutdown()
    time.sleep(2)
    can_bus = can.Bus(
        interface="socketcan",   # vector / pcan configurable
        channel="can0",
        bitrate=500000
    )
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, GW_STATUS_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) == 0

def test_tc_gw_status_003_edge_case_first_message_in_sequence(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, GW_STATUS_ID, 1)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    first_msg = msgs[0]
    assert decode_gateway_mode(first_msg) == 1
    assert decode_network_health(first_msg) == 0
    assert decode_alive_counter(first_msg) == 0

def test_tc_gw_status_004_edge_case_last_message_in_sequence(can_bus):
    # Simulate multiple messages sent in sequence
    for i in range(10):
        can_bus.send(can.Message(arbitration_id=GW_STATUS_ID, data=[1, 0, 0, 0]))
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, GW_STATUS_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    last_msg = msgs[-1]
    assert decode_gateway_mode(last_msg) == 1
    assert decode_network_health(last_msg) == 0
    assert decode_alive_counter(last_msg) == 0
Note that I've assumed the CAPL script is transmitting Gateway_Status messages with the following format:

* GatewayMode: bit 0-2 (7-bit value)
* NetworkHealth: bit 3-4 (3-bit value)
* AliveCounter: bit 5-8 (4-bit value)

You may need to adjust the decode functions accordingly if the actual message format is different.

import can
import pytest
import time

# Constants
BODY_STATUS_ID = 0x2DA
DOOR_FL_ID = BODY_STATUS_ID
DOOR_FR_ID = BODY_STATUS_ID + 1
DOOR_RL_ID = BODY_STATUS_ID + 2
DOOR_RR_ID = BODY_STATUS_ID + 3
LIGHT_SWITCH_ID = BODY_STATUS_ID + 4

# Session fixture: creates a CAN bus connection to be used in tests.
@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(
        interface="socketcan",   # vector / pcan configurable
        channel="can0",
        bitrate=500000
    )
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

# Function to collect messages from the CAN bus for a specified duration.
def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    messages = []
    start_time = time.time()
    while time.time() - start_time < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg is not None and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            messages.append(msg)
    return messages

# Function to decode signals from a CAN message.
def decode_door_fl(msg):
    """Extract DoorFL from CAN message. start_bit=0, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x1

def decode_door_fr(msg):
    """Extract DoorFR from CAN message. start_bit=1, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 1) & 0x1

def decode_door_rl(msg):
    """Extract DoorRL from CAN message. start_bit=2, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 2) & 0x1

def decode_door_rr(msg):
    """Extract DoorRR from CAN message. start_bit=3, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x1

def decode_light_switch(msg):
    """Extract LightSwitch from CAN message. start_bit=8, length=2, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[1] >> 0) & 0x3

# Test functions
@pytest.mark.parametrize("test_id", ["TC_001", "TC_002", "TC_003"])
def test_tc_gw_door(test_id, can_bus):
    # Collect messages for a specified duration.
    messages = collect_messages(can_bus, BODY_STATUS_ID, 2)

    # Assert received messages.
    assert len(messages) > 0

    # Decode signals from the CAN message.
    door_fl = decode_door_fl(messages[0])
    door_fr = decode_door_fr(messages[0])
    door_rl = decode_door_rl(messages[0])
    door_rr = decode_door_rr(messages[0])
    light_switch = decode_light_switch(messages[0])

    # Assert signal values.
    if test_id == "TC_001":
        assert door_fl == 1
        assert door_fr == 0
        assert door_rl == 1
        assert door_rr == 0
        assert light_switch == 0
    elif test_id == "TC_002":
        assert door_fl == 255
        assert door_fr == 255
        assert door_rl == 255
        assert door_rr == 255
        assert light_switch == 255
    elif test_id == "TC_003":
        # Edge case: first opened.
        pass

# Test cases for error conditions.
@pytest.mark.parametrize("test_id", ["TC_004"])
def test_tc_gw_door_error(test_id, can_bus):
    # Collect messages for a specified duration.
    messages = collect_messages(can_bus, BODY_STATUS_ID, 2)

    # Assert received messages.

response.python_code:
Here is the generated pytest test script based on the provided manual test cases:

import can
import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID = 0x2BC

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def decode_gatewaymode(msg):
    """Extract GatewayMode from CAN message. start_bit=0, length=3, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x7

def decode_networkhealth(msg):
    """Extract NetworkHealth from CAN message. start_bit=3, length=2, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x3

def decode_alivecounter(msg):
    """Extract AliveCounter from CAN message. start_bit=5, length=4, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 5) & 0xF

def test_tc_gateway_status_report_normal_operation(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) >= 1
    for m in msgs:
        assert decode_gatewaymode(m) == 0
        assert decode_networkhealth(m) == 0
        assert decode_alivecounter(m) == 0

def test_tc_missing_gateway_status_report(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) == 0

def test_tc_gateway_status_report_timing_edge(can_bus):
    # Note: This test case is not directly implementable as it requires a timing edge
    #       which cannot be simulated with the provided information.
    pytest.skip("Cannot simulate timing edge")
This script meets all the requirements specified, including:

1. Exact imports and constants.
2. CAN fixture with session scope.
3. Message collection utility function.
4. Decode helpers for GatewayMode, NetworkHealth, and AliveCounter signals.
5. Test functions for each manual test case, following the required naming format and structure.

Note that I've added a `pytest.skip` statement in the last test case (`test_tc_gateway_status_report_timing_edge`) as it's not directly implementable with the provided information.

# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Gateway Status Report Normal Operation

**Description**: Verify GatewayECU reports its status validity to ClusterECU under normal conditions.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'GatewayECU transmits Gateway_Status message', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set Gateway Mode to Normal'}
- {'action': 'ClusterECU receives Gateway_Status message', 'signal': 'GatewayStatusValid', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify Gateway Status Validity is reported as true'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU reports its status validity to ClusterECU successfully.

## TC_002: Missing Gateway Status Report

**Description**: Verify ClusterECU handles missing Gateway_Status message from GatewayECU.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Simulate missing Gateway_Status message from GatewayECU', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set Gateway Mode to Normal'}
- {'action': 'ClusterECU handles missing Gateway_Status message', 'signal': 'GatewayStatusValid', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify Gateway Status Validity is reported as false due to missing message'}

**Expected Result**: ClusterECU reports Gateway Status Validity as false when Gateway_Status message is missing.

## TC_003: Gateway Status Report Timing Edge

**Description**: Verify GatewayECU reports its status validity to ClusterECU at the minimum cycle time of 100 ms.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'GatewayECU transmits Gateway_Status message at minimum cycle time', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set Gateway Mode to Normal'}
- {'action': 'ClusterECU receives Gateway_Status message within 100 ms', 'signal': 'GatewayStatusValid', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify Gateway Status Validity is reported as true within minimum cycle time'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU reports its status validity to ClusterECU at the minimum cycle time of 100 ms.

## TC_004: Invalid Gateway Mode Signal Value

**Description**: Verify GatewayECU handles invalid GatewayMode signal value.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'GatewayECU transmits Gateway_Status message with invalid GatewayMode signal value', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 2, 'description': 'Set Gateway Mode to Invalid'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU reports its status validity to ClusterECU with invalid GatewayMode signal value.

## TC_005: First Gateway Status Report

**Description**: Verify GatewayECU reports its status validity to ClusterECU on the first transmission.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'GatewayECU transmits first Gateway_Status message', 'signal': 'GatewayMode', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Set Gateway Mode to Normal'}
- {'action': 'ClusterECU receives first Gateway_Status message', 'signal': 'GatewayStatusValid', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify Gateway Status Validity is reported as true on the first transmission'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU reports its status validity to ClusterECU successfully on the first transmission.

**Description**: Verify horn request state transmission when driver releases the horn switch

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def decode_gatewaymode(msg):
    """Extract GatewayMode from CAN message. start_bit=0, length=3, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x7

def decode_networkhealth(msg):
    """Extract NetworkHealth from CAN message. start_bit=3, length=2, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x3

def decode_alivecounter(msg):
    """Extract AliveCounter from CAN message. start_bit=5, length=4, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 5) & 0xF

def test_tc_001(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) >= 1
    for m in msgs:
        assert decode_gatewaymode(m) == 0
        assert decode_networkhealth(m) == 0
        assert decode_alivecounter(m) == 0

def test_tc_002(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) >= 1
    for m in msgs:
        assert decode_gatewaymode(m) != 0 or decode_networkhealth(m) != 0 or decode_alivecounter(m) != 0

def test_tc_003(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) >= 1
    for m in msgs:
        assert decode_gatewaymode(m) == 0
        assert decode_networkhealth(m) == 0
        assert decode_alivecounter(m) == 0

def test_tc_004(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) >= 1
    for m in msgs:
        assert decode_gatewaymode(m) != 0 or decode_networkhealth(m) != 0 or decode_alivecounter(m) != 0

def test_tc_005(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) >= 1
    for m in msgs:
        assert decode_gatewaymode(m) == 1

        assert decode_alivecounter(m) == 0
