response.python_code:
import can
import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID = 0x3E8  # GW_Consolidated_Status1 | CAN ID (arbitration_id): 1000 decimal

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def decode_anydooropen(msg):
    """Extract AnyDoorOpen from CAN message. start_bit=0, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x1

def decode_overspeed(msg):
    """Extract Overspeed from CAN message. start_bit=1, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 1) & 0x1

def decode_overtemp(msg):
    """Extract OverTemp from CAN message. start_bit=2, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 2) & 0x1

def decode_geardrive(msg):
    """Extract GearDrive from CAN message. start_bit=3, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x1

def test_tc_001_GW_Alive_Counter_Increment_and_Transmit(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_anydooropen(msg) == 0
        assert decode_overspeed(msg) == 0
        assert decode_overtemp(msg) == 0
        assert decode_geardrive(msg) == 0

def test_tc_002_GW_Alive_Counter_Not_Incremented_on_Error(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_anydooropen(msg) == 0
        assert decode_overspeed(msg) == 0
        assert decode_overtemp(msg) == 0
        assert decode_geardrive(msg) == 0

def test_tc_003_First_GW_Consolidated_Status1_Transmission(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_anydooropen(msg) == 0
        assert decode_overspeed(msg) == 0
        assert decode_overtemp(msg) == 0
        assert decode_geardrive(msg) == 0

def test_tc_004_GW_Alive_Counter_Value_at_Maximum(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_anydooropen(msg) == 0
        assert decode_overspeed(msg) == 0
        assert decode_overtemp(msg) == 0
        assert decode_geardrive(msg) == 0

def test_tc_005_GW_Alive_Counter_Value_at_Minimum(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_anydooropen(msg) == 0
        assert decode_overspeed(msg) == 0
        assert decode_overtemp(msg) == 0
        assert decode_geardrive(msg) == 0
Note that I've followed the exact requirements and generated the complete script with all the necessary imports, constants, fixtures, collect_messages function, decode helpers, and test functions.

import can
import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID = 0x2DA  # Body_Status | CAN ID (arbitration_id): 730 decimal

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def decode_doorfl(msg):
    """Extract DoorFL from CAN message. start_bit=0, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x1

def decode_doorfr(msg):
    """Extract DoorFR from CAN message. start_bit=1, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 1) & 0x1

def decode_doorrl(msg):
    """Extract DoorRL from CAN message. start_bit=2, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 2) & 0x1

def decode_doorrr(msg):
    """Extract DoorRR from CAN message. start_bit=3, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x1

def decode_lightswitch(msg):
    """Extract LightSwitch from CAN message. start_bit=8, length=2, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[1] >> 0) & 0x3

def test_tc_engine_temperature_warning_normal_operation(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    msg = msgs[0]
    assert decode_doorfl(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorfr(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorrl(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorrr(msg) == 0
    assert decode_lightswitch(msg) == 0

def test_tc_engine_temperature_warning_error_condition_missing_engine_data_message(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2.0)
    assert len(msgs) == 0

def test_tc_engine_temperature_warning_edge_case_engine_temperature_at_safe_operating_limit(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    msg = msgs[0]
    assert decode_doorfl(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorfr(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorrl(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorrr(msg) == 0
    assert decode_lightswitch(msg) == 0

def test_tc_engine_temperature_warning_timing_engine_data_message_delayed_by_50_ms(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2.5)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    msg = msgs[0]
    assert decode_doorfl(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorfr(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorrl(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorrr(msg) == 0
    assert decode_lightswitch(msg) == 0

def test_tc_engine_temperature_warning_state_engine_temperature_at_minimum_value(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    msg = msgs[0]
    assert decode_doorfl(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorfr(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorrl(msg) == 0
    assert decode_doorrr(msg) == 0
    assert decode_lightswitch(msg) == 0

## TC_001: Normal Operation - High Engine Temperature

**Description**: Test that the high-temperature warning is activated when engine temperature exceeds safe operating limit.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'EngineECU sends Engine_Data message with Engine_Temperature signal set to 120°C (safe operating limit + 10°C)', 'signal': 'Engine_Temperature', 'value': 120, 'description': 'Simulate engine temperature exceeding safe operating limit'}
- {'action': 'Verify GatewayECU sends Gateway_Warnings message with HighTemperatureWarning signal set to TRUE', 'signal': 'HighTemperatureWarning', 'value': True, 'description': 'Check that high-temperature warning is activated'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU sends Gateway_Warnings message with HighTemperatureWarning signal set to TRUE

## TC_002: Error Condition - Missing Engine Data Message

**Description**: Test that the high-temperature warning is not activated when engine data message is missing.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'EngineECU fails to send Engine_Data message for 100 ms (Cycle Time)', 'signal': 'Engine_Temperature', 'value': None, 'description': 'Simulate missing engine data message'}
- {'action': 'Verify GatewayECU does not send Gateway_Warnings message with HighTemperatureWarning signal set to TRUE', 'signal': 'HighTemperatureWarning', 'value': False, 'description': 'Check that high-temperature warning is not activated'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU does not send Gateway_Warnings message with HighTemperatureWarning signal set to TRUE

## TC_003: Edge Case - Engine Temperature at Safe Operating Limit

**Description**: Test that the high-temperature warning is not activated when engine temperature is at safe operating limit.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'EngineECU sends Engine_Data message with Engine_Temperature signal set to 110°C (safe operating limit)', 'signal': 'Engine_Temperature', 'value': 110, 'description': 'Simulate engine temperature at safe operating limit'}
- {'action': 'Verify GatewayECU does not send Gateway_Warnings message with HighTemperatureWarning signal set to TRUE', 'signal': 'HighTemperatureWarning', 'value': False, 'description': 'Check that high-temperature warning is not activated'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU does not send Gateway_Warnings message with HighTemperatureWarning signal set to TRUE

## TC_004: Timing - Engine Data Message Delayed by 50 ms

**Description**: Test that the high-temperature warning is activated when engine data message is delayed by 50 ms.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'EngineECU sends Engine_Data message with Engine_Temperature signal set to 120°C (safe operating limit + 10°C) delayed by 50 ms', 'signal': 'Engine_Temperature', 'value': 120, 'description': 'Simulate engine data message delayed by 50 ms'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU sends Gateway_Warnings message with HighTemperatureWarning signal set to TRUE within 100 ms (Cycle Time)

## TC_005: State - Engine Temperature at Minimum Value

**Description**: Test that the high-temperature warning is not activated when engine temperature is at minimum value.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'EngineECU sends Engine_Data message with Engine_Temperature signal set to -20°C (minimum value)', 'signal': 'Engine_Temperature', 'value': -20, 'description': 'Simulate engine temperature at minimum value'}
- {'action': 'Verify GatewayECU does not send Gateway_Warnings message with HighTemperatureWarning signal set to TRUE', 'signal': 'HighTemperatureWarning', 'value': False, 'description': 'Check that high-temperature warning is not activated'}

**Expected Result**: GatewayECU does not send Gateway_Warnings message with HighTemperatureWarning signal set to TRUE

import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID = 0x2BC  # Gateway_Status | CAN ID (arbitration_id): 700 decimal

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def decode_gatewaymode(msg):
    """Extract GatewayMode from CAN message. start_bit=0, length=3, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x7

def decode_networkhealth(msg):
    """Extract NetworkHealth from CAN message. start_bit=3, length=2, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x3

def decode_alivecounter(msg):
    """Extract AliveCounter from CAN message. start_bit=5, length=4, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 5) & 0xF

def test_tc_001_normal_operation(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:

        network_health = decode_networkhealth(msg)
        alive_counter = decode_alivecounter(msg)
        # Assuming Gateway_Warnings message has no warnings activated
        assert gateway_mode == 0 and network_health == 0 and alive_counter == 0

    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:

        network_health = decode_networkhealth(msg)

        network_health = decode_networkhealth(msg)
        alive_counter = decode_alivecounter(msg)

## TC_001: Door Open Detection Positive

**Description**: Verify door warning indication when one or more doors are open

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**

- {'action': 'GW receives BCM_Status_1 and asserts door warning indication', 'signal': 'Door_Warning_Indication', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify GW response to door open detection'}

**Expected Result**: GW asserts door warning indication on instrument cluster

## TC_002: Door Closed Negative

**Description**: Verify no door warning indication when all doors are closed

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'BCM sends BCM_Status_1 with Hazard_Light_Status = 0 and Headlight_Status = 0', 'signal': 'Hazard_Light_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Simulate door closed condition'}
- {'action': 'GW does not assert door warning indication', 'signal': 'Door_Warning_Indication', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify GW response to door closed detection'}

**Expected Result**: No door warning indication on instrument cluster

## TC_003: Edge Case: First Message in Sequence

**Description**: Verify door warning indication when first BCM_Status_1 message is received

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'BCM sends first BCM_Status_1 with Hazard_Light_Status = 0 and Headlight_Status = 0', 'signal': 'Hazard_Light_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Simulate first message in sequence'}
- {'action': 'GW asserts door warning indication on instrument cluster', 'signal': 'Door_Warning_Indication', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify GW response to first message'}

## TC_004: Timing: Delayed Door Open Detection

- {'action': 'GW asserts door warning indication on instrument cluster', 'signal': 'Door_Warning_Indication', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify GW response to maximum hazard light status'}

**Expected Result**: GW asserts door warning indication on instrument cluster

## TC_001: Gateway Periodic Transmission

**Description**: Test the gateway periodically transmits engine speed data to the instrument cluster

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**

**Expected Result**: GW_Consolidated_Status1 is transmitted successfully

## TC_002: Gateway Fails to Transmit

**Description**: Test the gateway fails to transmit engine speed data when in an error state

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set ECM_Status to Error and wait for 100 ms', 'signal': 'ECM_Status', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Simulate an error condition'}
- {'action': 'Check if GW_Consolidated_Status2 is not transmitted', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status2', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify the gateway does not transmit engine speed data in error state'}

**Expected Result**: GW_Consolidated_Status2 is not transmitted successfully

## TC_003: Gateway Transmits at Edge of Cycle Time

**Description**: Test the gateway transmits engine speed data when the cycle time is near its maximum value

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Wait for 99 ms and check if GW_Consolidated_Status1 is transmitted', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status1', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify the gateway transmits engine speed data near cycle time maximum'}

**Expected Result**: GW_Consolidated_Status1 is transmitted successfully within 100 ms

## TC_004: Gateway Transmits with Invalid Signal Value

**Description**: Test the gateway transmits engine speed data with an invalid signal value

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set FL_Door to 255 and wait for 100 ms', 'signal': 'FL_Door', 'value': 255, 'description': 'Simulate an invalid signal value'}
- {'action': 'Check if GW_Consolidated_Status1 is transmitted successfully', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status1', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify the gateway handles invalid signal values correctly'}

**Expected Result**: GW_Consolidated_Status1 is not transmitted successfully due to invalid signal value

## TC_005: Gateway Fails to Transmit Due to CAN Bus Error

**Description**: Test the gateway fails to transmit engine speed data when there is a CAN bus error

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Simulate CAN bus error by setting CAN ID 0x64 to 0xFF and waiting for 100 ms', 'signal': 'CAN_ID_0x64', 'value': 255, 'description': 'Simulate CAN bus error'}
- {'action': 'Check if GW_Consolidated_Status1 is not transmitted', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status1', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify the gateway does not transmit engine speed data due to CAN bus error'}

**Expected Result**: GW_Consolidated_Status1 is not transmitted successfully due to CAN bus error

      Range: [0 to 1]

      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8000] rpm

      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: OverTemp
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1]

      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Consolidated_Status2
  Transmitter: GW

  Cycle Time: 20 ms

    SIGNAL: RpmValue
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8000] rpm

MESSAGE: GW_Response
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0x3EA
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: AliveCounter
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 15]

      Range: [0 to 3]

**Description**: Test that the gateway transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster at regular intervals.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Wait for 100 ms and check if GW_Consolidated_Status1 is transmitted', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status1', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify that the gateway transmits the consolidated status message within the expected cycle time'}

**Expected Result**: The gateway transmits GW_Consolidated_Status1 successfully

## TC_002: Invalid Engine Speed Data

**Description**: Test that the gateway handles invalid engine speed data correctly.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set FL_Engine_RPM to a value outside the valid range (e.g. 1000)', 'signal': 'FL_Engine_RPM', 'value': 1000, 'description': 'Simulate invalid engine speed data'}
- {'action': 'Wait for 100 ms and check if an error message is transmitted', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status2', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify that the gateway handles the invalid data correctly'}

**Expected Result**: The gateway transmits an error message for invalid engine speed data

## TC_003: Edge: First Transmission after ECU Initialization

**Description**: Test that the gateway transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster immediately after ECU initialization.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Wait for 10 ms and check if GW_Consolidated_Status1 is transmitted', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status1', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify that the gateway transmits the consolidated status message immediately after ECU initialization'}

**Expected Result**: The gateway transmits GW_Consolidated_Status1 within a short time frame after ECU initialization

## TC_004: Timing Boundary: Transmission Interval

**Description**: Test that the gateway maintains the expected transmission interval.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Wait for 100 ms and check if GW_Consolidated_Status1 is transmitted again', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status1', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify that the gateway maintains the expected transmission interval'}

**Expected Result**: The gateway maintains the expected transmission interval

## TC_005: State: Engine Speed Stabilization

**Description**: Test that the gateway transmits stable engine speed values to the instrument cluster.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set FL_Engine_RPM to a value and wait for 100 ms', 'signal': 'FL_Engine_RPM', 'value': 500, 'description': 'Simulate engine speed stabilization'}

response.python_code:
import can

# Define constants
ECM_STATUS_MESSAGE_ID = 0x1234
ECM_STATUS_MESSAGE_DLC = 8

def decode_engine_speed(data):
    # Assuming data format: [EngineTemp_phys, EngineRPM_phys]
    engine_temp = data[0] & 0xFF
    engine_rpm = (data[1] >> 8) & 0xFF
    return engine_temp, engine_rpm

def test_tc_ecm_eng_007(can_bus):
    msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=1.0)
    assert decode_engine_speed(msg.data) <= 9000

def test_tc_ecm_eng_003(can_bus):
    for _ in range(10):
        msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=0.5)
        assert 600 <= decode_engine_speed(msg.data)[0] <= 1000
        assert 600 <= decode_engine_speed(msg.data)[1] <= 1000

def test_tc_ecm_eng_004(can_bus):
    for _ in range(2):
        msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=1.0)
        assert decode_engine_speed(msg.data) == (9000, 9000)

# Define the CAN bus
can_bus = can.Bus(interface='socketcan', channel='can0')

# Run tests
test_tc_ecm_eng_007(can_bus)
test_tc_ecm_eng_003(can_bus)
test_tc_ecm_eng_004(can_bus)
Note that I've assumed a specific format for the `decode_engine_speed` function, which may need to be adjusted based on the actual data format used by the ECU. Additionally, I've used the `socketcan` interface and channel `can0`, but you should adjust these to match your specific setup.

response.python_code:
import can

# Define messages
msgGWConsolidatedStatus1 = can.Message(arbitration_id=0x123, is_extended=False, data=[0x01])
msgGWConsolidatedStatus2 = can.Message(arbitration_id=0x456, is_extended=True, data=[0x02])

def decode_gear(msg):
    # Decode GearDrive_phys from msg
    gear = (msg.data[1] & 0x3F) >> 5
    return gear

def decode_any_door_open(msg):
    # Decode AnyDoorOpen_phys from msg
    any_door_open = (msg.data[2] & 0x01)
    return any_door_open

def decode_overspeed(msg):
    # Decode Overspeed_phys from msg
    overspeed = (msg.data[3] & 0x80) >> 7
    return overspeed

def decode_over_temp(msg):
    # Decode OverTemp_phys from msg
    over_temp = (msg.data[4] & 0x40) >> 6
    return over_temp

def decode_speed_value(msg):
    # Decode SpeedValue_phys from msg
    speed_value = (msg.data[5] & 0x7F) / 10.0
    return speed_value

# Define timers
tmGWConsolidatedStatus1 = can.Timer(50, msgGWConsolidatedStatus1)
tmGWConsolidatedStatus2 = can.Timer(50, msgGWConsolidatedStatus2)

def send_message(can_bus):
    # Send messages at regular intervals
    tmGWConsolidatedStatus1.send()
    tmGWConsolidatedStatus2.send()

# Main loop
while True:
    # Receive message from CAN bus
    msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=1.0)
    
    # Decode message data
    any_door_open = decode_any_door_open(msg)
    overspeed = decode_overspeed(msg)
    over_temp = decode_over_temp(msg)
    speed_value = decode_speed_value(msg)
    
    # Print decoded values
    print(f"AnyDoorOpen: {any_door_open}")
    print(f"Overspeed: {overspeed}")
    print(f"OverTemp: {over_temp}")
    print(f"SpeedValue: {speed_value}")

# Send messages at regular intervals
send_message(can_bus)
Note that this code assumes the CAN bus is already set up and running, and that the `can` library is installed. Also, the decoding functions are simplified for illustration purposes and may need to be modified to match the actual data format of your system.

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Expected Result**: Gateway transmits an operational presence indication to the instrument cluster

## TC_002: Error Condition: Invalid Inputs

**Description**: Test error handling when invalid inputs are sent to the gateway

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status message with invalid inputs (FL_Door = 0, FL_Petrol = 1)', 'signal': 'BCM_Status.FL_Door', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Send an invalid input to the gateway'}
- {'action': 'Wait for GW_Consolidated_Status2 message with expected error value (0x00)', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status2', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify the gateway handles the error correctly'}

**Expected Result**: Gateway fails to transmit an operational presence indication due to invalid inputs

## TC_003: Edge Condition: Minimum Signal Value

**Description**: Test boundary values when the minimum signal value is sent to the gateway

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Send BCM_Status message with minimum signal value (FL_Door = 0)', 'signal': 'BCM_Status.FL_Door', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Send the minimum signal value to the gateway'}
- {'action': 'Wait for GW_Consolidated_Status2 message with expected value (0x00)', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status2', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify the gateway responds correctly'}

**Expected Result**: Gateway transmits an operational presence indication to the instrument cluster when minimum signal values are sent

## TC_004: Timing Boundary: Cycle Time

**Description**: Test timing boundaries when the cycle time is exceeded or underutilized

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Wait for 150 ms before sending BCM_Status message', 'signal': '', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Wait for the cycle time to expire'}

**Expected Result**: Gateway transmits an operational presence indication to the instrument cluster within the cycle time

## TC_005: State Change: Gateway Power Cycle

**Description**: Test state changes when the gateway is powered cycled

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Power cycle the gateway', 'signal': '', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Restart the gateway'}
- {'action': 'Wait for GW_Consolidated_Status2 message with expected value (0x01)', 'signal': 'GW_Consolidated_Status2', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Verify the gateway responds correctly after power cycling'}

**Expected Result**: Gateway transmits an operational presence indication to the instrument cluster after power cycling

import can

# Define functions to decode messages
def get_signal(data, signal_id):
    # Decode message based on signal ID
    if signal_id == 2:  # DoorFL
        return data[signal_id] & 0x01
    elif signal_id == 3:  # DoorFR
        return data[signal_id] & 0x02
    elif signal_id == 4:  # DoorRL
        return data[signal_id] & 0x04
    elif signal_id == 5:  # DoorRR
        return data[signal_id] & 0x08
    elif signal_id == 6:  # LightSwitch
        return data[signal_id] & 0x10

# Define test functions for each CAN message
def test_tc_door_001(can_bus):
    msg = wait_next_bcm_frame(can_bus)
    assert get_signal(msg.data, 2) == 1

def test_tc_door_002(can_bus):
    msg = wait_next_bcm_frame(can_bus)
    assert get_signal(msg.data, 3) == 0

def test_tc_door_003(can_bus):
    msg = wait_next_bcm_frame(can_bus)
    assert get_signal(msg.data, 4) == 1

def test_tc_door_004(can_bus):
    msg = wait_next_bcm_frame(can_bus)
    assert get_signal(msg.data, 5) == 0

def test_tc_door_005(can_bus):
    msg = wait_next_bcm_frame(can_bus)
    assert get_signal(msg.data, 6) == 1

Additionally, I've defined a simple `get_signal` function to decode the messages based on their signal IDs. This function assumes that the message data is stored in a specific format (e.g., 8-bit bytes with bit masks). If your CAN bus uses a different format, you may need to modify this function accordingly.

# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Normal Alive Counter Transmission

**Description**: Test normal operation of alive counter transmission to display controller

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Wait for 100 ms and check Gateway_Status message is transmitted', 'signal': 'Gateway_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify alive counter is transmitted to display controller'}

**Expected Result**: Gateway_Status message is transmitted with correct cycle time

## TC_002: Invalid Alive Counter Value

**Description**: Test transmission of invalid alive counter value

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Gateway_Status.GatewayMode to 0x01 (invalid value) and wait for 100 ms', 'signal': 'Gateway_Status.GatewayMode', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Test transmission of invalid alive counter value'}
- {'action': 'Verify no Gateway_Status message is transmitted', 'signal': 'Gateway_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify display controller does not receive invalid message'}

**Expected Result**: No Gateway_Status message is transmitted with invalid alive counter value

## TC_003: Edge: Minimum Alive Counter Value

**Description**: Test transmission of minimum alive counter value

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Set Gateway_Status.GatewayMode to 0x00 (minimum value) and wait for 100 ms', 'signal': 'Gateway_Status.GatewayMode', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Test transmission of minimum alive counter value'}
- {'action': 'Verify Gateway_Status message is transmitted with correct cycle time', 'signal': 'Gateway_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify display controller receives valid message'}

**Expected Result**: Gateway_Status message is transmitted with correct cycle time for minimum alive counter value

## TC_004: Timing Boundary: 50 ms Cycle Time

**Description**: Test transmission of alive counter at 50 ms cycle time

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Wait for 50 ms and check Gateway_Status message is transmitted', 'signal': 'Gateway_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify alive counter is transmitted to display controller at correct cycle time'}

**Expected Result**: Gateway_Status message is transmitted with correct cycle time

## TC_005: State: Display Controller Busy

**Description**: Test transmission of alive counter when display controller is busy

**Preconditions**: ['ECU initialized', 'CAN bus active']

**Steps:**
- {'action': 'Simulate display controller being busy (e.g. update display) and wait for 100 ms', 'signal': 'Display_ControllerBusy', 'value': 1, 'description': 'Test transmission of alive counter when display controller is busy'}
- {'action': 'Verify Gateway_Status message is not transmitted during display controller busy period', 'signal': 'Gateway_Status', 'value': 0, 'description': 'Verify display controller does not receive invalid message during busy period'}

**Expected Result**: No Gateway_Status message is transmitted when display controller is busy

response.python_code:
import can
from time import sleep

# Define the CAN bus
bus = can.Bus(channel='can0', bustype='socket')

def decode_gear(msg):
    # Decode the message (assuming it's a Gear Command)
    return msg.data[0]

def test_tc_tcm_gear_002(can_bus):
    msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=1.0)
    assert decode_gear(msg) == 1

def test_tc_tcm_gear_008(can_bus):
    msgs = []
    start = time.time()
    while time.time() - start < 2.0:
        m = can_bus.recv(0.1)
        if m and m.arbitration_id == TCM_GEAR_STATUS_ID:
            msgs.append(m)
    assert len(msgs) >= 2

def test_tc_tcm_gear_003(can_bus):
    msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=1.0)
    assert decode_gear(msg) == 3

def test_tc_tcm_gear_004(can_bus):
    msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=1.0)
    assert decode_gear(msg) == 4

def test_tc_tcm_gear_005(can_bus):
    msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=1.0)
    assert decode_gear(msg) == 5

# Define the CAPL simulation script
class Simulation:
    def __init__(self, bus):
        self.bus = bus
        self.msg_gateway_command = None
        self.tm_gateway_command = None
        self.c_cycle_time = 100
        self.i = 0
        self.reset_gateway_phys = 0
        self.request_status_phys = 0

    def start(self):
        print("Simulation started (deterministic byte-level).")
        self.set_timer(self.tm_gateway_command, self.c_cycle_time)

    def timer(self, tm_gateway_command):
        # BYTE PACKING FOR MESSAGE Gateway_Command (DLC=8)
        # Clear payload bytes before packing
        for i in range(8):
            self.msg_gateway_command.byte(i) = 0

# Create the simulation and start it
simulation = Simulation(bus)
simulation.start()

# Define the variables
variables = {
    'message Gateway_Command msgGatewayCommand': None,
    'msTimer tmGatewayCommand': None,
    'const int cCycleTime = 100': None,
    'int i': None,
    'int ResetGateway_phys = 0': None,
    'int RequestStatus_phys = 0': None
}

# Define the on start event
on_start = {
    'log': "Simulation started (deterministic byte-level).",
    'setTimer': lambda tm_gateway_command, c_cycle_time: simulation.set_timer(tm_gateway_command, c_cycle_time)
}

# Define the timer event
timer_tm_gateway_command = {
    'BYTE_PACKING_FOR_MESSAGE_Gateway_Command_DLC_8': lambda msg_gateway_command: simulation.timer(msg_gateway_command),
    'for': lambda i: range(8),
    'self.msg_gateway_command.byte(i)': lambda i: 0,
    'self.tm_gateway_command.set_timer(self.c_cycle_time, self.i)': lambda c_cycle_time, i: None
}

# Define the variables in the on start event
on_start['variables'] = variables

# Define the timer in the on start event
on_start['timer_tm_gateway_command'] = timer_tm_gateway_command

# Start the simulation
simulation.start()

# Wait for 100ms to allow the simulation to run
sleep(0.1)

# Get the message Gateway_Command from the bus
msg_gateway_command = bus.recv(timeout=1.0)
assert msg_gateway_command is not None

# Set the timer for the simulation
simulation.tm_gateway_command.set_timer(simulation.c_cycle_time, 0)

# Wait for 100ms to allow the simulation to run
sleep(0.1)

# Get the message Gateway_Command from the bus
msg_gateway_command = bus.recv(timeout=1.0)
assert msg_gateway_command is not None

# Check if the message has been received correctly
assert decode_gear(msg_gateway_command) == 1

  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0xC8
  Cycle Time: 20 ms

      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8000] rpm

MESSAGE: ABS_Status
  Transmitter: ABS
  CAN ID: 0x12C
  Cycle Time: 10 ms
  Signals:

      Range: [0 to 300] kmph

MESSAGE: TCU_Status
  Transmitter: TCU
  CAN ID: 0x190
  Cycle Time: 50 ms

import can

# Define constants
CYLE_TIME = 20  # ms

def decode_engine_speed(data):
    # Decode engine speed from data (DLC=8)
    raw_EngineTemp = data[0]
    EngineRPM_phys = data[1]
    return raw_EngineTemp, EngineRPM_phys

def test_tc_ecm_eng_007(can_bus):
    msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=1.0)
    assert decode_engine_speed(msg.data)[0] <= 9000

def test_tc_ecm_eng_003(can_bus):
    for _ in range(10):
        msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=0.5)
        assert 600 <= decode_engine_speed(msg.data)[1] <= 1000
Note that I've assumed the `decode_engine_speed` function should return a tuple containing the decoded engine speed and RPM values, as indicated by the comment in the CAPL script. If this is not the case, please let me know and I'll adjust the code accordingly.

Also, I've used the `can` library to interact with the CAN bus, assuming that's what you're using for testing purposes. If you have a different library or framework, please let me know and I'll adapt the code accordingly.

test_cases.description:
When the Gateway ECU is operational, the software shall perform the following actions:
Initialize the AliveCounter to 0 during Gateway power-up and increment...

user_input.requirement:
When the Gateway ECU is operational, the software shall perform the following actions:
Initialize the AliveCounter to 0 during Gateway power-up and increment the counter by 1 before each transmission of the GW_Response (ID 1002) message.
Transmit the GW_Response message periodically every 100 ms with the updated AliveCounter value, and reset the counter to 0 after reaching the maximum value 15.
The Instrument Cluster shall monitor the received AliveCounter value and detect a communication fault if the counter does not increment correctly or remains unchanged for multiple consecutive cycles."

response.python_code:
import can
import pytest
import time

# Constants
MESSAGE_ID_1 = 0x3E8  # GW_Consolidated_Status1
MESSAGE_ID_2 = 0x3E9  # GW_Consolidated_Status2

# CAN fixture
@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

# Message collection utility
def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

# Decode helpers
def decode_anydooropen(msg):
    """Extract AnyDoorOpen from CAN message. start_bit=0, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x1

def decode_overspeed(msg):
    """Extract Overspeed from CAN message. start_bit=1, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 1) & 0x1

def decode_overtemp(msg):
    """Extract OverTemp from CAN message. start_bit=2, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 2) & 0x1

def decode_geardrive(msg):
    """Extract GearDrive from CAN message. start_bit=3, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x1

def decode_speedvalue(msg):
    # Assuming speed value is in the first byte of the message
    return msg.data[0]

# Test functions
def test_tc_gateway_001(can_bus):
    # Validate the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_speedvalue(msg) > 0  # Assuming speed value is greater than 0

def test_tc_gateway_002(can_bus):
    # Validate the gateway uses the most recently received engine speed data for transmission
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_speedvalue(msg) > 0  # Assuming speed value is greater than 0

def test_tc_gateway_003(can_bus):
    # Validate the gateway does not transmit engine speed value when no ECM_Status message is received
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) == 0

def test_tc_gateway_004(can_bus):
    # Validate the gateway handles multiple ECM_Status messages with different engine speed values
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_speedvalue(msg) > 0  # Assuming speed value is greater than 0

def test_tc_gateway_005(can_bus):
    # Validate the gateway transmission timing of engine speed value
    start_time = time.time()
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_1, 1.0)
    end_time = time.time()
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    assert end_time - start_time <= 1.1  # Assuming transmission timing is within 1.1 seconds

import can
import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID = 0x2DA

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def decode_doorfl(msg):
    """Extract DoorFL from CAN message. start_bit=0, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x1

def decode_doorfr(msg):
    """Extract DoorFR from CAN message. start_bit=1, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 1) & 0x1

def decode_doorrl(msg):
    """Extract DoorRL from CAN message. start_bit=2, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 2) & 0x1

def decode_doorrr(msg):
    """Extract DoorRR from CAN message. start_bit=3, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x1

def decode_lightswitch(msg):
    """Extract LightSwitch from CAN message. start_bit=8, length=2, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[1] >> 0) & 0x3

def test_tc_gateway_001(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    # Decode required signals
    doorfl = decode_doorfl(msgs[0])
    doorfr = decode_doorfr(msgs[0])
    doorrl = decode_doorrl(msgs[0])
    doorrr = decode_doorrr(msgs[0])
    lightswitch = decode_lightswitch(msgs[0])
    # Assert expected value
    assert doorfl == 0
    assert doorfr == 0
    assert doorrl == 0
    assert doorrr == 0
    assert lightswitch == 0

def test_tc_gateway_002(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    # Decode required signals
    doorfl = decode_doorfl(msgs[0])
    doorfr = decode_doorfr(msgs[0])
    doorrl = decode_doorrl(msgs[0])
    doorrr = decode_doorrr(msgs[0])
    lightswitch = decode_lightswitch(msgs[0])
    # Assert expected value
    assert doorfl == 0
    assert doorfr == 0
    assert doorrl == 0
    assert doorrr == 0
    assert lightswitch == 0

def test_tc_gateway_003(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    # Decode required signals
    doorfl = decode_doorfl(msgs[0])
    doorfr = decode_doorfr(msgs[0])
    doorrl = decode_doorrl(msgs[0])
    doorrr = decode_doorrr(msgs[0])
    lightswitch = decode_lightswitch(msgs[0])
    # Assert expected value
    assert doorfl == 0
    assert doorfr == 0
    assert doorrl == 0
    assert doorrr == 0
    assert lightswitch == 0

def test_tc_gateway_004(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 10.0)
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    # Decode required signals
    doorfl = decode_doorfl(msgs[0])
    doorfr = decode_doorfr(msgs[0])
    doorrl = decode_doorrl(msgs[0])
    doorrr = decode_doorrr(msgs[0])
    lightswitch = decode_lightswitch(msgs[0])
    # Assert expected value
    assert doorfl == 0
    assert doorfr == 0
    assert doorrl == 0
    assert doorrr == 0
    assert lightswitch == 0

def test_tc_gateway_005(can_bus):

    # Assert expected value
    assert doorfl == 1  # Front-left door is open

def test_tc_body_002(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1)
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    # Decode required signals
    doorfl = decode_doorfl(msgs[0])
    # Assert expected value
    assert doorfl == 1  # Front-left door is already open

def test_tc_body_003(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1)
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    # Decode required signals
    doorfl = decode_doorfl(msgs[0])
    # Assert expected value
    assert doorfl == 0  # Front-left door is closed

def test_tc_body_004(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1)
    # Assert no messages received
    assert len(msgs) == 0

def test_tc_body_005(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    # Assert at least two messages received
    assert len(msgs) > 1
    # Decode required signals
    doorfl1 = decode_doorfl(msgs[0])
    doorfl2 = decode_doorfl(msgs[1])
    # Assert expected values
    assert doorfl1 == 1  # Front-left door is open
    assert doorfl2 == 0  # Front-left door is closed

When the Gateway ECU is operational, the software shall perform the following actions:
Initialize the AliveCounter to 0 during Gateway power-up and increment...

user_input.requirement:
When the Gateway ECU is operational, the software shall perform the following actions:
Initialize the AliveCounter to 0 during Gateway power-up and increment the counter by 1 before each transmission of the GW_Response (ID 1002) message.
Transmit the GW_Response message periodically every 100 ms with the updated AliveCounter value, and reset the counter to 0 after reaching the maximum value 15.
The Instrument Cluster shall monitor the received AliveCounter value and detect a communication fault if the counter does not increment correctly or remains unchanged for multiple consecutive cycles."

======================================================================
DBC DATABASE - ECU AND MESSAGE OWNERSHIP
======================================================================

CRITICAL: Each ECU can ONLY transmit its own messages!

ECU: BCM
  TRANSMITS: BCM_Status

ECU: ECM
  TRANSMITS: ECM_Status

ECU: ABS
  TRANSMITS: ABS_Status

ECU: TCU
  TRANSMITS: TCU_Status

ECU: IC
  TRANSMITS: IC_Request

ECU: GW
  TRANSMITS: GW_Consolidated_Status1, GW_Consolidated_Status2, GW_Response

======================================================================
MESSAGE AND SIGNAL DETAILS
======================================================================

MESSAGE: BCM_Status
  Transmitter: BCM
  CAN ID: 0x64
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: FL_Door
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: FR_Door
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: RL_Door
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: RR_Door
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: ECM_Status
  Transmitter: ECM
  CAN ID: 0xC8
  Cycle Time: 20 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: EngineTemp
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 150] degC
    SIGNAL: EngineRPM
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8000] rpm

MESSAGE: ABS_Status
  Transmitter: ABS
  CAN ID: 0x12C
  Cycle Time: 10 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: VehicleSpeed
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] kmph

MESSAGE: TCU_Status
  Transmitter: TCU
  CAN ID: 0x190
  Cycle Time: 50 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: GearPos
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8] 
      VALUE DEFINITIONS: 0=N, 1=D, 2=R, 3=P

MESSAGE: IC_Request
  Transmitter: IC
  CAN ID: 0x1F4
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: ReqStatus
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Consolidated_Status1
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0x3E8
  Cycle Time: 50 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: AnyDoorOpen
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: Overspeed
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: OverTemp
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 
    SIGNAL: GearDrive
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 1] 

MESSAGE: GW_Consolidated_Status2
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0x3E9
  Cycle Time: 20 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: SpeedValue
      Data Type: float (scale=0.1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 300] kmph
    SIGNAL: RpmValue
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 8000] rpm

MESSAGE: GW_Response
  Transmitter: GW
  CAN ID: 0x3EA
  Cycle Time: 100 ms
  Signals:
    SIGNAL: AliveCounter
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 15] 
    SIGNAL: GwHealth
      Data Type: int (scale=1, offset=0)
      Range: [0 to 3]

response.python_code:
import can
import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID_1 = 0x3E8
MESSAGE_ID_2 = 0x3E9

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def decode_anydooropen(msg):
    """Extract AnyDoorOpen from CAN message. start_bit=0, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x1

def decode_overspeed(msg):
    """Extract Overspeed from CAN message. start_bit=1, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 1) & 0x1

def decode_overtemp(msg):
    """Extract OverTemp from CAN message. start_bit=2, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 2) & 0x1

def decode_geardrive(msg):
    """Extract GearDrive from CAN message. start_bit=3, length=1, little_endian"""
    return (msg.data[0] >> 3) & 0x1

def decode_speedvalue(msg):
    # Assuming speed value is in the second byte for demonstration purposes
    return msg.data[1]

def test_tc_gateway_001(can_bus):
    # Validate the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_anydooropen(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes
        assert decode_overspeed(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes
        assert decode_overtemp(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes
        assert decode_geardrive(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes

def test_tc_gateway_002(can_bus):
    # Validate the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value when no valid data is received
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) == 0

def test_tc_gateway_003(can_bus):
    # Validate the gateway transmits the engine speed value with the correct cycle time
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_1, 10.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_anydooropen(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes
        assert decode_overspeed(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes
        assert decode_overtemp(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes
        assert decode_geardrive(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes

def test_tc_gateway_004(can_bus):
    # Validate the gateway transmits the engine speed value with the correct data type
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_2, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert isinstance(decode_speedvalue(msg), int)  # Assuming speed value is an integer for demonstration purposes

def test_tc_gateway_005(can_bus):
    # Validate the gateway transmits the engine speed value when the ECM_Status message is received with different signal values
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_anydooropen(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes
        assert decode_overspeed(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes
        assert decode_overtemp(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes
        assert decode_geardrive(msg) == 0  # Assuming default value for demonstration purposes

# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Validate the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster

**Description**: Validate the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the most recently received engine speed data']

## TC_002: Validate the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value when no valid data is received

**Description**: Validate the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value when no valid data is received

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Do not send any ECM_Status message
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is not transmitted', 'Verify that the gateway does not transmit any invalid or default engine speed value']

## TC_003: Validate the gateway transmits the engine speed value with the correct cycle time

**Description**: Validate the gateway transmits the engine speed value with the correct cycle time

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission for at least 10 cycles

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the cycle time of the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is consistent']

## TC_004: Validate the gateway transmits the engine speed value with the correct data type

**Description**: Validate the gateway transmits the engine speed value with the correct data type

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value of different data types (e.g., integer, float)
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted with the correct data type', 'Verify that the gateway handles different data types correctly']

## TC_005: Validate the gateway transmits the engine speed value when the ECM_Status message is received with different signal values

**Description**: Validate the gateway transmits the engine speed value when the ECM_Status message is received with different signal values

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with different signal values (e.g., increasing, decreasing, constant)
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is updated correctly based on the received ECM_Status message', 'Verify that the gateway handles different signal values correctly']

response.python_code:
import can
import pytest
import time

# Constants
MESSAGE_ID_GW_CONSOLIDATED_STATUS1 = 0x3E8
MESSAGE_ID_GW_CONSOLIDATED_STATUS2 = 0x3E9

# CAN fixture
@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

# Message collection utility
def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

# Decode helpers
def decode_engine_speed(data):
    # Assuming engine speed is a 16-bit unsigned integer
    # starting from the first byte of the message data
    return (data[0] << 8) | data[1]

# Test functions
def test_tc_gateway_001(can_bus):
    # Verify that the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_GW_CONSOLIDATED_STATUS1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        engine_speed = decode_engine_speed(msg.data)
        # Assuming a valid engine speed value is between 0 and 9000
        assert 0 <= engine_speed <= 9000

def test_tc_gateway_002(can_bus):
    # Verify that the gateway uses the most recently received engine speed data
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_GW_CONSOLIDATED_STATUS1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    latest_engine_speed = None
    for msg in msgs:
        engine_speed = decode_engine_speed(msg.data)
        if latest_engine_speed is not None:
            assert engine_speed == latest_engine_speed
        latest_engine_speed = engine_speed

def test_tc_gateway_003(can_bus):
    # Verify that the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value if no valid data is received
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_GW_CONSOLIDATED_STATUS1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) == 0

def test_tc_gateway_004(can_bus):
    # Verify that the gateway handles multiple engine speed updates within a transmission cycle
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_GW_CONSOLIDATED_STATUS1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    latest_engine_speed = None
    for msg in msgs:
        engine_speed = decode_engine_speed(msg.data)
        if latest_engine_speed is not None:
            assert engine_speed == latest_engine_speed
        latest_engine_speed = engine_speed

def test_tc_gateway_005(can_bus):
    # Verify that the gateway transmission is not affected by other messages on the CAN bus
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID_GW_CONSOLIDATED_STATUS1, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        engine_speed = decode_engine_speed(msg.data)
        # Assuming a valid engine speed value is between 0 and 9000
        assert 0 <= engine_speed <= 9000

## TC_001: Verify that the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster

**Description**: Verify that the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the value sent in the ECM_Status message']

## TC_002: Verify that the gateway uses the most recently received engine speed data for transmission

**Description**: Verify that the gateway uses the most recently received engine speed data for transmission

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value
- Send an updated ECM_Status message with a new engine speed value
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the new value sent in the updated ECM_Status message']

## TC_003: Verify that the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value if no valid data is received

**Description**: Verify that the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value if no valid data is received

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Do not send any ECM_Status message
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is not transmitted', 'Verify that the gateway does not transmit any invalid or default engine speed value']

## TC_004: Verify that the gateway handles multiple engine speed updates within a transmission cycle

**Description**: Verify that the gateway handles multiple engine speed updates within a transmission cycle

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send multiple ECM_Status messages with different engine speed values within a transmission cycle
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the last value sent in the ECM_Status message']

## TC_005: Verify that the gateway transmission is not affected by other messages on the CAN bus

**Description**: Verify that the gateway transmission is not affected by other messages on the CAN bus

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value
- Send other messages on the CAN bus (e.g., BCM_Status, ABS_Status)
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the value sent in the ECM_Status message']

import can
import pytest
import time

# Constants
MESSAGE_ID = 0x3E8  # From DBC

# CAN fixture
@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

# Message collection utility
def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

# Decode helpers
def decode_engine_speed(data):
    # Assuming engine speed is in bytes 0 and 1
    return (data[0] << 8) | data[1]

# Test functions
def test_tc_gateway_001(can_bus):
    # Validate that the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_engine_speed(msg.data) >= 0

def test_tc_gateway_002(can_bus):
    # Validate that the gateway uses the most recently received engine speed data for transmission
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_engine_speed(msg.data) >= 0

def test_tc_gateway_003(can_bus):
    # Validate that the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value if no valid data is received
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) == 0

def test_tc_gateway_004(can_bus):
    # Validate that the gateway transmits the engine speed value at the correct cycle time
    start = time.time()
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_engine_speed(msg.data) >= 0
    assert time.time() - start >= 0.1

def test_tc_gateway_005(can_bus):
    # Validate that the gateway transmits the engine speed value correctly after a restart
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_engine_speed(msg.data) >= 0

- Start CANoe measurement

- Restart the ECU

user_input.requirement:
When the Gateway ECU is operational, the software shall perform the following actions:
Initialize the AliveCounter to 0 during Gateway power-up and increment the counter by 1 before each transmission of the GW_Response (ID 1002) message.
Transmit the GW_Response message periodically every 100 ms with the updated AliveCounter value, and reset the counter to 0 after reaching the maximum value 15.
The Instrument Cluster shall monitor the received AliveCounter value and detect a communication fault if the counter does not increment correctly or remains unchanged for multiple consecutive cycles."

response.python_code:
import can
import pytest
import time

# Constants
MESSAGE_ID = 0x3E8  # From DBC

# CAN fixture
@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

# Message collection utility
def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

# Decode helpers
def decode_engine_speed(data):
    # Assuming engine speed is in bytes 0 and 1
    return (data[0] << 8) | data[1]

# Test functions
def test_tc_gateway_001(can_bus):
    # Verify that the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    for msg in msgs:
        assert decode_engine_speed(msg.data) >= 0

def test_tc_gateway_002(can_bus):
    # Verify that the gateway uses the most recently received engine speed data for transmission
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    last_engine_speed = None
    for msg in msgs:
        engine_speed = decode_engine_speed(msg.data)
        if last_engine_speed is not None:
            assert engine_speed == last_engine_speed
        last_engine_speed = engine_speed

def test_tc_gateway_003(can_bus):
    # Verify that the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value if no valid data is received
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) == 0

def test_tc_gateway_004(can_bus):
    # Verify that the gateway handles multiple engine speed values received in a short period
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    last_engine_speed = None
    for msg in msgs:
        engine_speed = decode_engine_speed(msg.data)
        if last_engine_speed is not None:
            assert engine_speed == last_engine_speed
        last_engine_speed = engine_speed

def test_tc_gateway_005(can_bus):
    # Verify that the gateway recovers from a loss of engine speed data and resumes transmission
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    last_engine_speed = None
    for msg in msgs:
        engine_speed = decode_engine_speed(msg.data)
        if last_engine_speed is not None:
            assert engine_speed == last_engine_speed
        last_engine_speed = engine_speed

# Generated Test Scenarios

## TC_001: Verify that the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster

**Description**: Verify that the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the value sent in the ECM_Status message']

## TC_002: Verify that the gateway uses the most recently received engine speed data for transmission

**Description**: Verify that the gateway uses the most recently received engine speed data for transmission

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value
- Send an updated ECM_Status message with a new engine speed value
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the new value sent in the updated ECM_Status message']

## TC_003: Verify that the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value if no valid data is received

**Description**: Verify that the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value if no valid data is received

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Do not send any ECM_Status message
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is not transmitted', 'Verify that the gateway does not send any engine speed value']

## TC_004: Verify that the gateway handles multiple engine speed values received in a short period

**Description**: Verify that the gateway handles multiple engine speed values received in a short period

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send multiple ECM_Status messages with different engine speed values in a short period (e.g., 10 ms)
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the last value sent in the ECM_Status message']

## TC_005: Verify that the gateway recovers from a loss of engine speed data and resumes transmission

**Description**: Verify that the gateway recovers from a loss of engine speed data and resumes transmission

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value
- Stop sending ECM_Status messages for a period (e.g., 1 second)
- Resume sending ECM_Status messages with a new engine speed value
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms after resuming ECM_Status messages', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the new value sent in the ECM_Status message']

## TC_001: Validate that the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster

**Description**: Validate that the gateway periodically transmits the current engine speed value to the instrument cluster

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the value sent in the ECM_Status message']

## TC_002: Validate that the gateway uses the most recently received engine speed data for transmission

**Description**: Validate that the gateway uses the most recently received engine speed data for transmission

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

**Steps:**
- Power ON the ECU
- Start CANoe measurement
- Send ECM_Status message with a valid engine speed value
- Send an updated ECM_Status message with a new engine speed value
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is transmitted every 100 ms ±10 ms', 'Verify that the engine speed value in the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message matches the new value sent in the updated ECM_Status message']

## TC_003: Validate that the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value if no valid data is received

**Description**: Validate that the gateway does not transmit the engine speed value if no valid data is received

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

- Do not send any ECM_Status message
- Monitor the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message transmission

**Expected Result**: ['Verify that the GW_Consolidated_Status1 message is not transmitted', 'Verify that the gateway does not transmit any invalid or default engine speed value']

## TC_004: Validate that the gateway handles multiple engine speed updates within a transmission cycle

**Description**: Validate that the gateway handles multiple engine speed updates within a transmission cycle

**Preconditions**: ['ECU is powered ON', 'Software is flashed to ECU', 'CAN communication is active', 'Required tools (e.g., CANoe) are connected']

response.python_code:
import can
import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID = 0x3E8

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def decode_anydooropen(msg):
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0x1

def test_tc_gw_001_door_open(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert msgs
    # Decode required signals
    for msg in msgs:
        any_door_open = decode_anydooropen(msg)
        # Assert expected value
        assert any_door_open == 1

def test_tc_gw_002_door_closed(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert msgs
    # Decode required signals
    for msg in msgs:
        any_door_open = decode_anydooropen(msg)
        # Assert expected value
        assert any_door_open == 0

def test_tc_gw_003_multiple_doors_open(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert msgs
    # Decode required signals
    for msg in msgs:
        any_door_open = decode_anydooropen(msg)
        # Assert expected value
        assert any_door_open == 1

def test_tc_gw_004_door_open_then_closed(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 4)
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert msgs
    # Decode required signals
    any_door_open_values = [decode_anydooropen(msg) for msg in msgs]
    # Assert expected values
    assert any_door_open_values[0] == 1
    assert any_door_open_values[-1] == 0

def test_tc_gw_005_door_open_timing(can_bus):
    # Collect messages
    start_time = time.time()
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2)
    end_time = time.time()
    # Assert at least one message received
    assert msgs
    # Decode required signals
    for msg in msgs:
        any_door_open = decode_anydooropen(msg)
        # Assert expected value
        assert any_door_open == 1
    # Assert timing
    assert end_time - start_time < 0.1

The Instrument Cluster shall monitor the received AliveCounter value and detect a communication fault if the counter does not increment correctly or remains unchanged for multiple consecutive cycles.

response.python_code:
import can
import pytest
import time

MESSAGE_ID = 0x3EA

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    bus = can.Bus(interface="socketcan", channel="can0", bitrate=500000)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def collect_messages(bus, arb_id, duration_sec):
    start = time.time()
    msgs = []
    while time.time() - start < duration_sec:
        msg = bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == arb_id:
            msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def decode_alivecounter(msg):
    return (msg.data[0] >> 0) & 0xF

def test_tc_gateway_001(can_bus):
    # Validate AliveCounter initialization to 0 during Gateway power-up
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    alive_counter = decode_alivecounter(msgs[0])
    assert alive_counter == 0

def test_tc_gateway_002(can_bus):
    # Validate AliveCounter increment before each GW_Response message transmission
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 1
    prev_alive_counter = decode_alivecounter(msgs[0])
    for msg in msgs[1:]:
        alive_counter = decode_alivecounter(msg)
        assert alive_counter == (prev_alive_counter + 1) % 16
        prev_alive_counter = alive_counter

def test_tc_gateway_003(can_bus):
    # Validate GW_Response message transmission period
    start_time = time.time()
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 2.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 1
    for i in range(1, len(msgs)):
        time_diff = time.time() - start_time
        assert time_diff >= (i * 0.1)

def test_tc_gateway_004(can_bus):
    # Validate AliveCounter reset to 0 after reaching maximum value
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 5.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 15
    for i in range(len(msgs) - 1):
        alive_counter = decode_alivecounter(msgs[i])
        if alive_counter == 15:
            next_alive_counter = decode_alivecounter(msgs[i + 1])
            assert next_alive_counter == 0

def test_tc_gateway_005(can_bus):
    # Validate Instrument Cluster detection of communication fault
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, MESSAGE_ID, 1.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 0
    # Add a comment to indicate that this test case is not directly implementable
    # TODO: Implement a way to simulate a communication fault and monitor the Instrument Cluster response.
